hl4NXSiLFio.title: If You Love The Lord, You Will Love His House
hl4NXSiLFio.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at First Love Church
hl4NXSiLFio.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that if you truly love God, you must love His house (the church) as God's love language, using the example of King Solomon's many wives to illustrate how understanding someone's specific love language is essential to truly demonstrating love. He emphasizes that regular, committed church attendance every Sunday is the primary evidence of loving God, warning that those who don't prioritize being "planted" in God's house rather than merely "visiting" will gradually fall away from spiritual devotion and strength. Through practical illustrations and scriptural references, he challenges Christians to spend quality time in God's presence at church, asserting that such consistent devotion results in spiritual growth and strength.

BT88rRaZ_dE.title: How You Can Preach Salvation: INTERACTION | Holy Ghost Encounter
BT88rRaZ_dE.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at his church in Accra, Ghana
BT88rRaZ_dE.summary: Bishop Heward-Mills teaches the foundational principles of soul-winning through personal interaction and friendship, emphasizing that Christians must engage people outside the church building as laborers in the harvest field. He outlines eight practical steps for interaction: making a decision to talk to strangers, being friendly and kind, maintaining a smiling face, spending quality time with people, avoiding unresponsive individuals, showing genuine interest in others' needs, overcoming hypocrisy through diverse relationships, and asking basic questions. The core message is that soul-winning is the art of friendship—building authentic relationships as Jesus modeled—rather than immediate preaching, as iron sharpens iron through peer influence.

UqoduxaIcSk.title: Seven Great Principles Of A Great Change Part 2 | Holy Ghost Encounter | Joshua Heward-Mills
UqoduxaIcSk.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaching Part 2 of 'Seven Great Principles of a Great Change' at Holy Ghost Encounter service
UqoduxaIcSk.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills explains that after being born again, great change comes through listening to preaching, where God's word is manifested alive through the power of the Holy Spirit. He emphasizes that preaching produces miracles, deliverance from evil spirits, and transformation—citing biblical examples like Philip's preaching in Samaria. He concludes with the principle that changing friends is essential to changing one's life, as bad company corrupts good character, and believers should seek relationships centered on God's truth.

uJhfjMv2fNU.title: Seven Great Principles Of A Great Change | Holy Ghost Encounter | Joshua Heward-Mills
uJhfjMv2fNU.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at First Love Church
uJhfjMv2fNU.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that genuine Christian change requires spiritual growth and maturity, not mere willpower or environmental changes. He emphasizes that being saved and being changed are different—the inner man must grow from spiritual infancy to adulthood to resist temptation and develop godly character. God's purpose is to conform believers into the image of Jesus through spiritual maturation, which transforms conduct, behavior, and witness to the world.

d7UaN0cOgiQ.title: John 3:16 | Holy Ghost Encounter | Joshua Heward-Mills
d7UaN0cOgiQ.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at his local church
d7UaN0cOgiQ.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills expounds on John 3:16, emphasizing God's greatness as the ultimate inventor and creative force, and His incomparable love demonstrated through Christ's sacrifice for sinners. He contrasts human love and earthly greatness with God's divine love, which pursues and accepts people unconditionally despite their sin and rebellion. The sermon calls listeners to recognize this unprecedented love and surrender to God's purpose and grace.

7tg4kpOci1o.title: Jesus And The Man Sick With Palsy | Holy Ghost Encounter | Joshua Heward-Mills
7tg4kpOci1o.preacherNote: Video confirmed as Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills (BJosh)
7tg4kpOci1o.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills preaches on the man sick with palsy (Mark 2), using the physical condition as a prophetic picture of spiritual bondage—being stuck in sin and lacking self-control. He emphasizes that salvation requires preaching and that people need faith and community (the four carriers) to break through obstacles and reach Jesus; transformation is not by personal willpower alone but through encountering Christ's power and surrendering to Him.

7vboHLP_A4Q.title: Honour For The Anointing | Holy Ghost Encounter | Joshua Heward-Mills
7vboHLP_A4Q.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching on honor, respect, and the anointing.
7vboHLP_A4Q.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches on honoring those who are due honor in life, emphasizing that honor means recognizing and respecting the anointing and calling of God on people. He identifies multiple levels of honor—from simple recognition of God's anointing to deeper forms of respect—and applies this to honoring parents, widows, employers, spiritual leaders, and prophets. The sermon concludes by establishing that the first level of honor is to recognize the anointing on a person, moving beyond knowing them "after the flesh" to perceiving the spiritual authority and grace of God operating through them.

JMXSpK9ez_M.title: The Good Samaritan | Holy Ghost Encounter | Joshua Heward-Mills
JMXSpK9ez_M.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching on church growth series, specifically on loving God's church and the parable of the Good Samaritan as a picture of salvation.
JMXSpK9ez_M.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills uses the parable of the Good Samaritan to illustrate salvation through Jesus Christ and emphasizes that loving God requires loving His church and its members. He explains that the Samaritan represents Jesus, who saw mankind wounded by sin, had compassion, and applied both wine (representing the blood) and oil (representing the Holy Spirit) for healing and transformation. The sermon stresses that Christians must not only be saved but must actively work for God by equipping others and building His church through discipleship and evangelism.

_jdXiNBge7g.title: Come Unto Me | Holy Ghost Encounter | Joshua Heward-Mills
_jdXiNBge7g.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at what appears to be his home church (Oasis International Centre or similar)
_jdXiNBge7g.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that Jesus offers simple rest to all who labor and carry heavy burdens—the solution is to "come unto me." He emphasizes that coming to Jesus means engaging with His word, praying, attending church, serving, and seeking pastoral counsel, rejecting Satan's complication of Christian simplicity. The message calls believers to stop overcomplicating their faith and relationship with God, instead finding Him through straightforward obedience and presence in His house.

IzXdXXuHACg.title: Reasons Why You Must Have A Mega Church - Part 1 | Holy Ghost Encounter | Joshua Heward-Mills
IzXdXXuHACg.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at his church
IzXdXXuHACg.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills introduces a series on why churches must embrace a mega-church vision, arguing that having large congregations is the most appropriate vision for Christians operating under Holy Spirit guidance. He emphasizes that true spiritual transformation comes through interaction with the Holy Spirit, which changes believers' ambitions from worldly pursuits to God's kingdom priorities, and that the church faces a critical need for laborers to reach the plentiful spiritual harvest. The sermon challenges believers to move beyond self-focused Christianity and prioritize God's work, illustrating that genuine Holy Spirit infilling produces a burden for souls and church growth.

8WKqMkTdUiE.title: Obedience The Master Key To The Presence Of God | Holy Ghost Encounter | Joshua Heward-Mills
8WKqMkTdUiE.keyScripture: John 8:29 - 'He that sent me is with me: for I do always those things that please him.'
8WKqMkTdUiE.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that obedience is the master key to experiencing God's continuous presence, contrasting intermittent encounters with sustained fellowship. He emphasizes that the Holy Spirit's presence is conditional upon constant obedience to God's instructions in daily life, using Jesus Christ and Enoch as examples of those who maintained unbroken communion with God. The sermon stresses that without God's presence, Christians lack the power to overcome sin and live holy lives, making obedience—not just occasional spiritual experiences—essential to the Christian walk.

xs7r0BdIvok.title: Jesus And The Woman With The Issue of Blood | Holy Ghost Encounter | Joshua Heward-Mills
xs7r0BdIvok.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching; video title confirms identity
xs7r0BdIvok.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches on the woman with the issue of blood, emphasizing that true transformation requires personal encounter with Jesus Christ himself, not merely hearing about him or knowing people around him. He illustrates that believers must push past the 'crowd'—religious activities, church structures, and other people—to make direct contact with Jesus through the Holy Spirit. He stresses that complete wholeness requires touching Jesus personally, accessing his voice through Scripture and the Holy Spirit, and developing an intimate relationship with God that changes one's spirit, soul, and body.

SQF3uyprmQ0.title: Make Yourselves Saviours of Men (The Last Words Of Jesus Christ) | Holy Ghost Encounter
SQF3uyprmQ0.preacherNote: BJosh preaching; includes personal testimonies and anecdotes
topics: Spirit of the world vs Holy Spirit
True heart revealed in final moments
SQF3uyprmQ0.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches on the last words of Jesus Christ before His ascension, emphasizing that Acts 1:8 reveals the Great Commission: believers receive power through the Holy Spirit to become witnesses. He contrasts the Spirit of God with the spirit of the world, arguing that true Christians are driven by the Holy Spirit to lead people to Jesus, not by worldly concerns. Through multiple personal near-death experiences, he illustrates how final moments reveal what is truly important in life—ultimately, soul-winning and making oneself a savior of men.

4DYspwS_-lY.title: Prayer Service | Holy Ghost Encounter | Joshua Heward-Mills
4DYspwS_-lY.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills leads a prayer service based on Revelation 8, emphasizing intercessory prayer and spiritual warfare.
4DYspwS_-lY.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills conducts a prayer service centered on Revelation 8, teaching that prayer mixed with worship ascends to God as incense and returns with divine responses: voices, thundering, lightning, and earthquakes. The congregation prays against three specific spiritual issues—degeneration, love of the world, and fleshly desires—and concludes with intercessory prayer for unsaved family members and the lost. The sermon emphasizes that prayer is not merely practical but a demonstration of faith in the spiritual realm where God actively transforms lives and answers the cries of His people.

tWMDX7dSbPI.title: Jesus And Blind Bartimaeus | Holy Ghost Encounter | Joshua Heward-Mills
tWMDX7dSbPI.preacherNote: Sermon title confirmed in video title and transcript opening
tWMDX7dSbPI.summary: Jesus encountered blind Bartimaeus on the roadside, representing every spiritually blind person's journey to receiving salvation. The sermon uses Bartimaeus's cry for mercy despite social pressure and the crowds' opposition as a model for how individuals must respond to Jesus passing by—publicly confessing faith despite worldly influences and peer disapproval. Bishop Heward-Mills emphasizes that salvation is a moment of divine encounter that requires deliberate, audible response to God's call, warning that delayed response and continual disobedience harden the heart toward the gospel.

yjaJWg3rsTA.title: Why Every Christian Must Pray | Holy Ghost Encounter | Joshua Heward-Mills
yjaJWg3rsTA.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills explains why every Christian must pray, not just pastors, using Isaiah 40:31 to show that prayer renews spiritual strength and enables believers to keep pace with God's movement. He addresses the question many face—'what profit will prayer bring?'—by demonstrating through scripture and personal testimony that prayerlessness weakens believers spiritually, making them vulnerable to demonic attack, while prayer strengthens them to resist the devil. The sermon emphasizes that spiritual battles are not against flesh and blood but against principalities, powers, and rulers of darkness, requiring active prayer and resistance rather than passive ignorance.

Tsl-gUxlzR0.title: Speaking In Tongues | Holy Ghost Encounter | Joshua Heward-Mills
Tsl-gUxlzR0.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at his church
Tsl-gUxlzR0.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that speaking in tongues is the baptism of the Holy Spirit promised by Jesus, enabling believers to pray according to God's will rather than their limited understanding. He explains that tongues allow direct communication with God, provide spiritual awareness of the unseen realm, and magnify God's power in our lives. The practice is essential for effective intercession, defense against spiritual attacks, and discernment of God's purposes.

bOQFBeuXjm8.title: What Happens When You Do Not Pray | Holy Ghost Encounter | Joshua Heward-Mills
bOQFBeuXjm8.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching on prayer and its consequences.
bOQFBeuXjm8.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that failure to pray leaves one vulnerable to depression, anxiety, and hopelessness, as prayer is the mechanism through which God changes circumstances and defeats demonic opposition. He illustrates through biblical example (King Ahab) and personal testimony that spiritual outcomes are determined through persistent prayer in the unseen realm, not by chance or natural effort alone. Without prayerful engagement, believers surrender control of their lives and circumstances to spiritual forces of darkness.

KUFOYKlblvM.title: Prayer | Holy Ghost Encounter | Joshua Heward-Mills
KUFOYKlblvM.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills teaching on prayer and the Lord's Prayer model
KUFOYKlblvM.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches a comprehensive prayer model based on the Lord's Prayer, emphasizing that Christians should pray daily starting with hallowing God's name, praying for His kingdom, aligning with His will, requesting daily bread (both physical and spiritual), and confessing sins. He instructs believers to follow Jesus's simple yet transformative prayer structure rather than complex methodologies, demonstrating how proper prayer molds character, increases sensitivity to the Holy Spirit, and provides specific topics for intercession throughout each day.

pEk2KZtjx7o.title: If You Love The Lord | Holy Ghost Encounter | Joshua Heward-Mills
pEk2KZtjx7o.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaching on loving God as the greatest commandment
topics: Marriage as illustration of relationship with God
pEk2KZtjx7o.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that loving God with all your heart, soul, and mind is the greatest commandment, using marriage as a primary illustration of how God desires exclusive, intimate, emotional devotion from His people. He warns against spiritual adultery—idolatry through love of money, status, worldly pleasures, and relationships that compete with God's preeminence—emphasizing that true love for God produces feelings, passion, and joy that cannot be manufactured through mere religious duty.

GB0WIb4wjhQ.title: The Art Of Following Solomon - Part 3 | Holy Ghost Encounter | Joshua Heward-Mills
GB0WIb4wjhQ.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching on wisdom, life principles, and God's way of solving problems through Scripture.
GB0WIb4wjhQ.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that following Solomon's example means seeking God's wisdom and His way of doing things rather than worldly methods. He emphasizes that God's wisdom is found in Scripture and applies to every life situation—marriage, work, finances, and relationships—though it often doesn't make logical sense. True prosperity comes through service in the church, yielding to authority, choosing character over appearance, and trusting God's tested methods found in His Word.

awfnGUok6WY.title: The Art Of Following Solomon | Holy Ghost Encounter | Joshua Heward-Mills
awfnGUok6WY.preacherNote: Video title confirms Joshua Heward-Mills as preacher; auto-captions transcribed with minimal punctuation.
awfnGUok6WY.summary: Jesus teaches financial prosperity through five principles: don't obsess over life, food, body, or clothing, recognizing that life is more than provision and the body more than appearance. He illustrates this through ravens (who neither sow nor reap yet are fed by God) and lilies (who don't labor yet are clothed in glory), then references Solomon's wealth as evidence that God recognizes and honors earthly prosperity. The art of following Solomon involves building God's house with a willing heart, coupled with diligence and labor, because giving toward God's kingdom releases blessing and demonstrates the supernatural principle that those who honor God with resources experience divine multiplication.

60oZhw6Junw.title: How To Be A Spiritual Christian - Part 3 | Holy Ghost Encounter | Joshua Heward-Mills
60oZhw6Junw.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching Part 3 of 'How To Be A Spiritual Christian' series
60oZhw6Junw.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that becoming a spiritual Christian requires seeking and submitting to God's will rather than following the vanity of one's own mind. He emphasizes that God has detailed plans for each believer's life that lead to an 'expected end' of blessing and satisfaction, contrasting this with the unspiritual person who makes decisions based solely on personal reasoning and preferences. Through teaching on heart transformation (Ezekiel 36:26), he reveals that true spirituality involves receiving a new heart that desires what God desires, leading to complete satisfaction and the fulfillment of God's perfect plan.

7Iv1QkoYNMQ.title: How To Be A Spiritual Christian - Part 2 | Holy Ghost Encounter | Joshua Heward-Mills
7Iv1QkoYNMQ.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching Part 2 of 'How To Be A Spiritual Christian' series at his church
7Iv1QkoYNMQ.summary: Joshua teaches that becoming a spiritual Christian requires deliberate decisions and changes in associations; the second way to become spiritual is to avoid keeping company with sinners and unbelievers, as you inevitably learn the ways and adopt the character of those you spend time with. He emphasizes personal responsibility, uses the Prodigal Son as an example of delayed repentance, and warns that 'evil communication' (worldly influences from friends, media, and groups) corrupts spirituality and limits personal growth in God.

GqMX2RKNsQU.title: Stir It Up : Loving God | Holy Ghost Encounter | Joshua Heward-Mills
topics: Stir Up the Gift of God
GqMX2RKNsQU.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills preaches on the power of loving God by examining what provoked Jesus to perform His greatest miracle—raising Lazarus from the dead. Using the account of Mary anointing Jesus with costly perfume (recorded in Matthew 26, Mark 14, and Luke 7), he contrasts genuine love for God with mere religious service, demonstrating that intimacy with God—expressed through devotion, sacrifice, and worship—moves God to supernatural action. He concludes that becoming a lover of God transforms one's life and invokes God's favor in ways that work, prayer, or achievement alone cannot.

p1cH_SoKU4k.title: Stir It Up : Obedience | Holy Ghost Encounter | Joshua Heward-Mills
p1cH_SoKU4k.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching a new sermon series titled 'Stir It Up'
topics: Stirring up the Gift of God
Holy Spirit as the Second Comforter
Jesus as Express Image of God
p1cH_SoKU4k.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills begins a new sermon series on stirring up the gift of God (the Holy Spirit) within believers. He explains that Jesus Christ is the expressed image of God's personality, and the Holy Spirit is 'another comforter' of the exact same kind, requiring the same understanding of what provokes and moves God. The central teaching is that divine provision and blessing come not from the severity of our problems, but from obedient obedience to specific instructions God gives us, illustrated through the miracle at Cana where servants filled water pots and obeyed Jesus' direction.

aWxLgRartCs.title: How To Become a Fruitful Christian - Part 1 | Holy Ghost Encounter | Joshua Heward-Mills
topics: Being planted in a local church
aWxLgRartCs.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that true Christian fruitfulness requires deep, permanent closeness to God through abiding in Him, comparing spiritual intimacy to physical union in marriage. He emphasizes that drawing near to God requires continuous personal effort—daily prayer, Bible study, and time alone with God—and that faithfulness must be demonstrated in all relationships and commitments, not just church attendance. The measure of discipleship is not external religiosity but the fruit produced, which only comes through remaining planted in a local church and maintaining unwavering loyalty to God and covenant relationships.

VyzvoQHUSpI.title: Be Led by the Spirit of God - Joshua Heward-Mills
VyzvoQHUSpI.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaching on being led by the Spirit of God
topics: Being led by the Holy Spirit
The anointing of the Holy Spirit
VyzvoQHUSpI.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that being led by the Holy Spirit transforms believers into sons and daughters of God and is the foundational principle for Christian life and fruitfulness. Unlike creation which continuously obeys God, humanity since Genesis 3 has sought independence; salvation restores the possibility of being continuously led by the indwelling Holy Spirit through obedience to His voice. The ability to hear and follow God's voice is the primary skill that determines spiritual growth, and continuous obedience to the Spirit's guidance—not intermittent religious practices—is the key to experiencing God's presence and transforming one's life.

d6ItkJWpaxQ.title: Flow with the Holy Spirit as a Person - Joshua Heward-Mills
topics: Power to live as a Christian
d6ItkJWpaxQ.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that the Holy Spirit must be understood and approached as a person—not merely a force or influence—with distinct characteristics including will, mind, emotions, and knowledge. He emphasizes that learning to flow with and relate to the Holy Spirit personally is essential for every Christian to experience God's reality and receive power to live a transformed life. The sermon introduces the foundational concept that the Holy Spirit is God Himself dwelling with believers, and establishes the framework for a series of teachings on how to develop an intimate, ongoing relationship with Him.

s99_dVGKyvU.title: The River of the Spirit - Joshua Heward-Mills
s99_dVGKyvU.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at what appears to be a camp or conference setting
s99_dVGKyvU.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that the Holy Spirit must be understood not only as a person but as a river—a supernatural force that flows where it chooses and cannot be controlled by human logic or planning. He emphasizes that true spiritual maturity requires surrendering personal agendas, fears, and understanding to allow the Spirit to direct all decisions, illustrated through biblical narratives like Peter walking on water and the Israelites receiving water from the rock. The message challenges believers to abandon themselves completely to God's supernatural direction in ministry, relationships, career, and daily life, accepting that this surrender often contradicts natural wisdom and involves stepping into uncertainty.

I--Gz6zPzow.title: Accessing the Holy Spirit (I Will Come to You) - Joshua Heward-Mills
I--Gz6zPzow.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching; he mentions arriving late from picking up 'Prophet' (likely his father Bishop Dag Heward-Mills)
topics: Gifts and power of the Spirit
I--Gz6zPzow.keyScripture: John 14:18 - I will come to you
I--Gz6zPzow.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches on recognizing the presence and manifestation of the Holy Spirit through physical and tangible evidence rather than mere doctrinal knowledge. He emphasizes that believers must understand spiritual seasons, wait patiently on God, and expect genuine supernatural encounters—including demonstrations of power, healings, visions, and emotional responses—as confirmation that the Spirit has truly arrived. Drawing from church history and personal testimony, he challenges believers to move beyond intellectual understanding and seek authentic, manifest presence of God in their lives.

ddTWzhy1uys.title: Not By Might GEM Chapel   - Joshua Heward-Mills
ddTWzhy1uys.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at GEM Chapel, celebrating Pastor Abin's birthday
topics: The anointing of the Holy Spirit
Mountains of opposition overcome by anointing
Not by might nor by power
Comparison of Jesus' carpenter phase to spiritual transformation
The Holy Spirit as comforter and indweller
ddTWzhy1uys.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills emphasizes that human achievement and spiritual transformation depend not on natural strength, education, or power, but entirely on the anointing and indwelling of the Holy Spirit. He illustrates how earthen vessels (corrupt, weak, naturally dishonorable humans) become instruments of God's glory through the Spirit's presence, comparing Jesus' transformation from carpenter to savior with believers' potential. The message calls listeners to embrace the reality of God's Spirit upon, with, and in them as the only means to overcome life's mountains and fulfill divine purpose.

CmvGVYOqLnc.title: The Sweet Influence of the Holy Spirit on your Obedience(Osu) -Joshua Heward-Mills
topics: Knowing Jesus as Lord not just Savior
CmvGVYOqLnc.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that the Holy Spirit's influence on obedience requires knowing Jesus Christ not merely as Savior and Friend, but as Master and Lord—a relationship that enables believers to escape worldly pollution and experience God's blessing. He emphasizes that without submission to Christ's lordship and instructions through the Holy Spirit, disobedience opens doors to Satan's interference in all areas of life. The sermon culminates in prayer ministry for healing and the infilling of the Holy Spirit as the essential power to transform lives and fulfill God's will.

sfOxNH9eU7w.title: Quench Not The Holy Spirit (Maritime)  - Joshua Heward-Mills
sfOxNH9eU7w.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at Maritime region
sfOxNH9eU7w.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that quenching the Holy Spirit occurs when believers fail to recognize and respond properly to the Holy Spirit's manifestations, resulting in interrupted divine flow and missed blessings. He explains that the Holy Spirit is gentle and easily offended, and outlines how ignorance, wrong responses, and neglect of His guidance—such as ignoring words of wisdom, knowledge, and faith—effectively silence His work in our lives. The solution is to cultivate spiritual awareness, recognize the Holy Spirit's presence, and actively cooperate with His manifestations through obedience and faith.

7KRLIAcbNaw.title: Where The Fire Goes Out (Ho)  - Joshua Heward-Mils
7KRLIAcbNaw.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at First Love Hope Church
topics: Baptism with the Holy Ghost and fire
Power of God in the church
7KRLIAcbNaw.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaches on why the fire of God goes out in churches, identifying three essential "woods" that fuel spiritual fire: speaking in tongues, aggressive soul-winning and gospel preaching, and signs and wonders. He warns that the modern church has lost its fire through respectability, hypocrisy (the leaven of the Pharisees), abandoning outreach, and prioritizing comfort over spiritual power. He challenges pastors and believers to return to authentic Holy Spirit-filled ministry marked by signs, wonders, and fervent prayer.

xHl7yyYgLU0.title: 7 Steps to the Anointing WIS - Joshua Heward-Mills
topics: The anointing of the Holy Spirit
The role of the Holy Spirit in ministry
The difference between favor and anointing
xHl7yyYgLU0.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches the essential importance of personal anointing by the Holy Spirit for effective ministry, using the account of Samson to illustrate how dependence on external things rather than God's presence leads to defeat. He contrasts Jesus and the apostles who waited for the Holy Spirit's empowerment before beginning their ministry, emphasizing that favor, wisdom, education, and good reputation are not substitutes for the indwelling power of the Spirit. The sermon stresses that believers must personally encounter God in their own 'upper room experience' and continuously depend on the Holy Spirit rather than on their own abilities or past successes.

ilBFn1sL4mQ.title: How to Multiply the Influence of the Holy Spirit  - Joshua Heward-Mills
ilBFn1sL4mQ.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at Foster Homes church
topics: Being led by the Holy Spirit
ilBFn1sL4mQ.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches how to increase and multiply the influence of the Holy Spirit in your life by moving away from flesh-led decisions toward Spirit-led living. He emphasizes that throughout Scripture every mistake has resulted from flesh overcoming spirit, and that transformation happens through desire, hunger for God, and talking about the Holy Spirit to stir up spiritual appetite. The core principle is that serving God requires the Holy Spirit's influence and control, not human effort or fleshly methods.

jnHzKYpjaDw.title: The Holy Spirit (Korle Bu) - Joshua Heward Mills
jnHzKYpjaDw.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at Korle Bu
topics: Holy Spirit as person not force
Difference between gifts and presence of Holy Spirit
Holy Spirit as comforter and counselor
jnHzKYpjaDw.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills emphasizes that true transformation comes not from witnessing miracles or signs of the Holy Spirit's power, but from a personal relationship and encounter with the Holy Spirit himself as a person. He contrasts knowing about the Holy Spirit's gifts and manifestations with actually knowing the Holy Spirit intimately, using his own encounter at a Benny Hinn meeting as a catalyst for seeking deeper relationship with the Spirit. The message calls believers to invite the Holy Spirit to become their constant companion, teacher, and counselor rather than a distant force.

EoQKZsUw9EA.title: Understanding The Holy Spirit (Dansoman) - Joshua Heward-Mills
EoQKZsUw9EA.preacherNote: Preached at Dansoman, thanking Pastor Frank and church leadership for the invitation.
topics: Blockages to the Spirit (lying, anger, bitterness)
EoQKZsUw9EA.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaches on understanding and receiving the Holy Spirit, emphasizing that God has already provided all good gifts to believers but they remain hidden until the Spirit reveals them. He teaches that spiritual hunger and genuine desire for the Holy Spirit—not mere intellectual acknowledgment—are essential to receiving His anointing and guidance. The sermon calls believers to abandon reliance on human solutions and instead seek the Spirit's direction for every aspect of life, from personal decisions to discovering God's calling and provision.

QjSp7Y5vxCI.title: Four Ways You Can Receive the Anointing - Joshua Heward-Mills
QjSp7Y5vxCI.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at what appears to be a North Carolina church service with healing ministry and teaching on the anointing.
topics: Four ways to receive the anointing
The role of vessels in ministry
Impartation and transmission of spiritual gifts
QjSp7Y5vxCI.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches the four primary methods through which believers receive the Holy Spirit's anointing and power: laying on of hands, anointing oil, sacred garments/mantles, and divine impartation. Using medical analogies (different routes of administering antibiotics) and biblical examples from Jesus's ministry, he emphasizes that the Holy Spirit operates through multiple channels, all validated by Scripture and demonstrated throughout redemptive history. The sermon stresses that spiritual sensitivity to these conduits—particularly through touch, anointing substances, and consecrated garments—is essential for receiving God's supernatural gifts and transformation.

hNQ-tjXiD1g.title: What is the Manifestation of the Holy Spirit - Joshua Heward-Mills
hNQ-tjXiD1g.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching on the manifestation of the Holy Spirit
topics: Signs and demonstrations of the Spirit
The testimony of the Holy Spirit
hNQ-tjXiD1g.summary: The manifestation of the Holy Spirit is the visible sign, expression, and demonstration of God's presence given to every believer for their profit and spiritual edification. The Holy Spirit works invisibly throughout our lives, but His manifestation occurs when He steps from the invisible into the visible realm—through signs, voices, feelings of presence, or other tangible experiences that confirm His reality and strengthen our faith. Without the manifestation of the Spirit and personal encounters with God's presence, Christians remain spiritually dead, disconnected from genuine relationship with the living God, and unable to overcome sin or experience true worship.

UAsrqlA7tMQ.title: 01 What Can Influence You  - Joshua Heward-Mills
UAsrqlA7tMQ.preacherNote: Video title confirms Joshua Heward-Mills as preacher
UAsrqlA7tMQ.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills introduces a series on the four influences affecting human decisions: the Holy Spirit, demons, earthly desires, and the flesh. He emphasizes that the Holy Spirit is not merely a force but a person (John 14:16) whose presence brings joy, transforms character, and reveals God's superior purpose for believers' lives. The sermon calls believers to receive and respond to the Holy Spirit's influence rather than being controlled by depression, worldly wisdom, and demonic influences, promising that spiritual transformation produces a completely new life.

BQce7G-EZyc.title: 03 Sweet Influences of The Holy Spirit on Your Destiny   Joshua Heward-Mills
BQce7G-EZyc.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills speaking at a camp setting
topics: Lust of the flesh, eyes, and pride of life
Being justified and glorified by God
The bride of Christ responding to God's call
BQce7G-EZyc.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills challenges believers at a camp to recognize the Holy Spirit's call to full-time ministry and service to God, arguing that worldly ambitions, sensuality, and demonic influences prevent people from responding to this divine calling. He emphasizes that those truly filled with the Holy Spirit will sense the pressure and tension of God's call and must choose between serving God completely or pursuing secular careers and education. Using the framework of God's foreknowledge, predestination, calling, justification, and glorification, he asserts that refusing the Spirit's call means one does not truly know God or possess the Holy Spirit.

KNi1bsFpcqY.title: 02 Sweet Influences of The Holy Spirit on Your Knowledge of God - Joshua Heward-Mills
KNi1bsFpcqY.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at a camp/conference setting
KNi1bsFpcqY.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that knowing God requires the Holy Spirit's influence and guidance, not human wisdom or worldly knowledge. He emphasizes that the Spirit of Truth reveals God's character and speaks through Scripture, dreams, conscience, and divine leading—enabling believers to have personal communion with God rather than merely intellectual understanding. The sermon challenges listeners to prioritize developing their relationship with God through the Holy Spirit over earthly accomplishments, wisdom, strength, or riches.

RkxBnoHjzIw.title: 04 Sweet Influences of The Holy Spirit on Your Becoming A Pastor   Joshua Heward Mills
RkxBnoHjzIw.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at a pastoral training camp
topics: Works of the flesh vs. fruit of the Spirit
RkxBnoHjzIw.summary: Bishop Joshua challenges believers, particularly pastoral candidates, that the true evidence of the Holy Spirit's work is the ability to oversee a flock—a tangible responsibility for discipling and shepherding people. He contrasts genuine pastoral calling with worldly pursuits (banking, law, entertainment) and warns that without actual sheep to oversee, one does not possess the Holy Spirit for ministry. Through scriptural exhortation on Galatians 5 and the works of the flesh, he calls young leaders to abandon sensuality and materialism, commit fully to pastoral work, and plant churches as evidence of the Spirit's transformative power.

NbGvEfeZF0U.title: 05 Sweet Influences of The Holy Spirit On Your Ability To Love Forgive -  Joshua Heward-Mills
NbGvEfeZF0U.preacherNote: Video title confirms Joshua Heward-Mills; auto-captions transcribed with minimal punctuation
NbGvEfeZF0U.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that the Holy Spirit's influence produces genuine love and forgiveness as essential markers of spiritual maturity and effective ministry. True love transcends natural human bonds (family, romance, self-interest) and extends to strangers and enemies, distinguishing the Spirit-filled believer from those operating in mere carnal emotion. Unforgiveness is equated with spiritual death, murder, and walking in darkness—disqualifying one from genuine Christianity regardless of other spiritual gifts or works.

-RZSfesDRcQ.title: 06 Sweet Influences of The Holy Spirit On Your Life Ministry Emphasis   - Joshua Heward-Mills
-RZSfesDRcQ.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching on the Holy Spirit's influence on life emphasis and ministry priorities.
topics: Prophecy and gifts of the Spirit
Personal calling and separation for God's work
-RZSfesDRcQ.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that when the Holy Spirit comes upon believers, it fundamentally shifts their life emphasis toward soul-winning, evangelism, and making disciples rather than personal pursuits like prosperity, marriage, career advancement, or material possessions. He contrasts the emphasis of his generation of pastors (prosperity, programs, prophecies about miracles) with the true emphasis the Spirit should create—witnessing and gospel preaching. He challenges believers to recognize that their main emphasis in life reveals which spirit they have, calling them to prioritize God's kingdom work above all other endeavors.

gLnsRMvuxa0.title: 07 Sweet Influences of The Holy Spirit On Your Words  - Joshua Heward-Mills
gLnsRMvuxa0.preacherNote: Video title confirms Joshua Heward-Mills as speaker
topics: Difference between natural and spiritual speech
gLnsRMvuxa0.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that the Holy Spirit imparts supernatural power and authority to the words Christians speak, distinguishing anointed, Spirit-empowered speech from mere eloquent or persuasive language. He emphasizes that true ministry operates through spiritual demonstration—the spirit behind words—rather than excellent oratory or man's wisdom, citing examples of Jesus commanding nature and apostles whose words brought transformation. Believers must be filled with the Holy Spirit through prayer and speaking in tongues to ensure their words carry divine authority and effectiveness in ministry, evangelism, and daily life.

xwoWyGTyCmI.title: 08 Sweet Influences of The Holy Spirit On Your Character  - Joshua Heward-Mills
xwoWyGTyCmI.note: Transcript quality too degraded by auto-caption errors to confidently identify preacher or extract sermon content

cV0LQfebors.title: 10 Sweet Influences of The Holy Spirit on Your Productivity  - Joshua Heward-Mills
cV0LQfebors.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching on the Holy Spirit's influence on productivity
cV0LQfebors.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches on how the sweet influence of the Holy Spirit transforms productivity and ministry, using Isaiah 32 to illustrate the contrast between barren wilderness and fruitful fields. He emphasizes that spiritual transformation comes not through human effort, helpers, or education, but through prayer for the Holy Spirit's anointing and power. The sermon demonstrates that when believers invite the Spirit's influence, their churches and businesses supernaturally shift from emptiness to abundance and fruitfulness.

g7FNE9EGSsg.title: 11 How To Multiply The Effects of The Holy Spirit - Joshua Heward-Mills
topics: Multiplying the effects of the Holy Spirit
Stirring up the gift of God
g7FNE9EGSsg.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches how to multiply the influence of the Holy Spirit in one's life through five practical methods: stirring up interest and hunger by talking and preaching about the Holy Spirit, praying specifically for the Holy Spirit, organizing prayer meetings, speaking to oneself in songs and hymns, and maintaining spiritual hunger. He emphasizes that consistent discussion and meditation on the Holy Spirit builds desire for His presence and power, just as Acts 4:31 demonstrates how prayer meetings release the Holy Spirit's boldness in believers.

JHCqxHunCDM.title: Phanerosis Part 2 | Meeting God | Joshua Heward-Mills
JHCqxHunCDM.preacherNote: Video title confirms 'Joshua Heward-Mills'; transcript shows consistent first-person teaching style matching BJosh's known ministry.
JHCqxHunCDM.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches on the manifestation of the Holy Spirit through vocal gifts, particularly speaking in tongues, distinguishing between tongues as a sign to unbelievers, tongues for private intercession and prayer, and tongues with interpretation for church edification. He clarifies biblical controversies regarding whether all Christians speak in tongues and whether interpretation is always required in corporate worship, emphasizing that some tongues are meant only for communication with God while others require interpretation for the church's benefit. The teaching demonstrates how the Holy Spirit empowers believers to pray beyond human understanding and to minister the gifts of the Spirit for the profit of all.

V422cqs37Tg.title: Phanerosis | Meeting God | Joshua Heward-Mills
V422cqs37Tg.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills (BJosh) preaching at a Friday night 'Phanerosis' (manifestation) service
V422cqs37Tg.summary: Joshua teaches on 'Phanerosis'—the visible manifestation of the Holy Spirit's presence given to every believer to profit spiritually and evangelistically. He explains biblical reasons why people fall under God's power: the glory of God filling a place, Jesus being revealed, divine transformation occurring, and angelic ministry at work. The message emphasizes that supernatural encounters with God's presence are biblical, necessary for true spiritual change, and should be expected in genuine Christian ministry.

4Z_AU_Iqw3Q.title: The Presence of the Holy Spirit - Joshua Heward-Mills
4Z_AU_Iqw3Q.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at his local church after a one-year break from ministry.
topics: The Holy Spirit as a person
Fellowship and communion with the Holy Spirit
Attributes of God: omniscience, omnipotence, omnipresence, eternality
The presence of God versus God's manifest presence
Continuous presence of the Holy Spirit
4Z_AU_Iqw3Q.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that the Holy Spirit is not a force but a person with whom believers must have active fellowship and communion. He emphasizes that walking in the Spirit requires continuous acknowledgment and relationship with the Holy Spirit, illustrated through personal examples of how this transforms marriages, relationships, and spiritual life. The glory of God's presence brings life, change, and transformation—moving believers from spiritual death to vibrant, living faith.

vvJsIpwp7dM.title: The Presence of the Holy Spirit Pt 2 - Joshua Heward-Mills
vvJsIpwp7dM.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching 'The Presence of the Holy Spirit Pt 2'
topics: Divine presence and conviction of sin
vvJsIpwp7dM.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches on the transformative presence of the Holy Spirit, emphasizing that God's goodness experienced in His presence is what sustains believers and leads to genuine repentance and change. He illustrates how knowing God's character and names—particularly Elohim, the Creator who calls things from nothing—transforms believers from emptiness to purpose and prevents spiritual collapse. The sermon stresses that without experiencing God's presence, Christians remain shallow, struggling with pride and sin, but those who encounter His goodness find satisfaction, joy, and the strength to endure in faith.

KWoaiY1loco.title: What Every Christian Should Know About Supernatural Things - Joshua Heward-Mills
KWoaiY1loco.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at his church
KWoaiY1loco.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaches that authentic Christianity must include the supernatural power of God—signs, wonders, healing, and deliverance—not merely doctrinal belief. He emphasizes that spiritual maturity through God's Word is essential to perceive and receive supernatural manifestations, and warns against a "form of godliness" that denies the power of God. The kingdom of God is characterized by the Holy Spirit's tangible intervention in believers' lives, confirming Scripture through miraculous demonstration.

UBStolOOL2U.title: The Laws of the Supernatural - Joshua Heward-Mills
UBStolOOL2U.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at his church
UBStolOOL2U.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches on the supernatural laws that govern God's power in believers' lives, emphasizing that understanding and obeying these laws enables God's power to flow. The two primary laws he focuses on are Divine Selection—God's sovereign choice of individuals for blessing and ministry—and Humility—the essential heart condition before God that precedes honor and supernatural provision. He contrasts natural physical laws (Newton's Laws, gravity) with spiritual laws, arguing that as believers learn to operate according to spiritual principles, they will experience the supernatural power of God in areas like healing, marriage, provision, and guidance.

d0T0h6BYs4Q.title: The Presence of God Pt 1 - Joshua Heward-Mills
d0T0h6BYs4Q.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at He Gave Gifts Unto Men Conference, First Love Church Austin
topics: Death to life through God's glory
Experiencing God versus knowing about God
d0T0h6BYs4Q.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that the presence of God—not just knowledge about Him—is essential for every Christian's spiritual life and transformation. He emphasizes that the Holy Spirit is a person with intellect, will, and preferences who desires intimate fellowship with believers, and that experiencing God's glory in His presence produces real transformation that knowledge and external practices alone cannot achieve. The message challenges Christians to move beyond distant worship to intimate encounter with God's presence, which brings the glory that changes us from death to life and makes us increasingly like Christ.

RRJycni98Ug.title: The Presence of God Pt 2 - Joshua Heward-Mills
RRJycni98Ug.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at First Love London church
topics: Knowing God vs knowing about God
RRJycni98Ug.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills emphasizes that the most important aspect of Christian life is developing a personal, intimate relationship with God's presence rather than just performing external religious duties. Drawing from Moses' encounter at Mount Sinai and Mary sitting at Jesus' feet, he teaches that knowing God personally—through spending time in His presence—is more valuable than any church activity or position. Without this foundational spiritual intimacy, believers become vulnerable to falling away from faith because they know about God through others' teaching but have never encountered Him themselves.

r36QZxl4sM4.title: The Presence of God Pt 3 - Joshua Heward-Mills
r36QZxl4sM4.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at First Love Church, London, UK. This is Part 3 of 'The Presence of God' series.
r36QZxl4sM4.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches on the presence of God, emphasizing that the Holy Spirit is a person (not merely power) who abides with believers forever as the Comforter, providing grace (undeserved help through teaching), mercy, and guidance. He contrasts spiritual orphanhood with the adopted status believers receive through the Holy Spirit's indwelling presence, illustrating how a father's guidance shapes one's life direction. The central message is that intimate fellowship with the Holy Spirit—not merely salvation or gifts—defines the Christian's protection from being left comfortless and powerless.

bg4IBXgG3A4.title: Organs of Perception - Joshua Heward-Mills
bg4IBXgG3A4.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at Jim Chapel (Pastor Aban's church); mentions his father Bishop David Heward-Mills and his pastors/leaders from First Love Church in attendance.
bg4IBXgG3A4.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches on developing spiritual organs of perception—the Christian's ability to sense God's presence, hear His voice, and experience the supernatural through the Holy Spirit rather than natural senses. Using Jacob's ladder vision as the primary example, he explains that believers must silence their flesh and enter stillness to access divine encounters, as the natural man cannot perceive spiritual reality. The central message is that God is constantly present and speaking, but most Christians miss it because their spiritual senses are dormant or undeveloped.

hf_uglIhxt4.title: Sweet Influence of the Holy Spirit to Assure You - Joshua Heward-Mills
topics: False apostles, prophets, evangelists, pastors, teachers
hf_uglIhxt4.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that God's Holy Spirit provides a seal, earnest, and assurance that confirms authentic Christianity, just as logos authenticate products. He explains that God's presence can be discerned through its aura—an intangible but recognizable atmosphere—and through hearing God's voice, which are more reliable indicators of His approval than material blessings. The sermon warns against counterfeits in the church: false apostles, prophets, evangelists, pastors, and teachers, teaching believers to seek the Holy Spirit's genuine presence rather than mere power, performance, or external success.

l8Hjhhl7KAY.title: Laws of Fruitfulness - Joshua Heward-Mills
l8Hjhhl7KAY.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching from his father Bishop Dag Heward-Mills' book 'Fruitfulness'
l8Hjhhl7KAY.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches the first law of fruitfulness from his father's book, emphasizing that God expects every Christian to bear fruit by leading people to Christ and assuming spiritual responsibility for them. He explains that branches failing to bear fruit are taken away from the vine (Jesus), resulting in disconnection from God's presence—the greatest consequence a believer can face. The sermon centers on how a Christian's nearness to God and access to His presence, mercy, and peace depend entirely on their fruitfulness in kingdom work.

ADH05ejjLTQ.title: 1. What Exactly is the Anointing- Joshua Heward-Mills
ADH05ejjLTQ.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching on the nature and power of the anointing
scriptures: Genesis 28 (Jacob and the stone at Bethel)
ADH05ejjLTQ.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills explains that the anointing is not an oil or technique but God's actual presence and person upon a minister. He illustrates through examples (Billy Graham vs. Reinhard Bonnke, personal anecdotes about church growth) that the Holy Spirit as a person—not methods or loyalty—is the secret behind spiritual effectiveness and ministry transformation. The true sign of the anointing is an addictive divine presence that creates a desire in people to encounter God repeatedly and develop their own relationship with Him.

ISLJxAqwAPw.title: 2 Where Exactly is the Anointing- Joshua Heward-Mills
ISLJxAqwAPw.preacherNote: Speaker identifies as 'Joshua' and 'be Josh' throughout; title confirms 'Joshua Heward-Mills'
topics: The anointing within, with, and upon believers
Three dimensions of the Holy Spirit
Church growth and the Holy Spirit
The anointing in Old Testament vs New Testament
ISLJxAqwAPw.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills explores where the anointing is located, defining it not as oil or substance but as God Himself dwelling within, with, and upon believers. He traces the biblical progression from Old Testament sanctuary worship to New Testament indwelling through Jesus's death and resurrection, showing three distinct dimensions of the Holy Spirit's presence: the anointing within (at salvation), the presence with us, and the power upon us for ministry. The sermon emphasizes that recognizing God in anointed leaders requires spiritual discernment, and that the corporate anointing of the Church—as the body of Christ—demonstrates God's power far more than individual manifestations.

9CfpkXE-3Fs.title: 3. How to Identify the Anointing Part 1- Joshua Heward-Mills

hgmwYcs52O0.title: 4 How to Identify the Anointing Part 2- Joshua Heward-Mills
hgmwYcs52O0.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills teaching from 'How to Identify the Anointing Part 2'
hgmwYcs52O0.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches how to identify anointed ministers by recognizing peculiar characteristics and spiritual distinctives in their ministry. He uses the Emmaus road account (Luke 24) as a primary example, showing how the disciples recognized Jesus by His peculiar manner of breaking bread and teaching Scripture. The sermon emphasizes that true anointing produces edification, exhortation, and comfort through words empowered by the Holy Spirit, not merely through external factors like music, charisma, or methodology.

o7L2HysmI58.title: 5 It is the Anointing That Breaks the Yoke- Joshua Heward-Mills
topics: The rod as symbol of anointing
o7L2HysmI58.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that it is not the anointed man himself but the anointing (the Holy Spirit's power) upon him that breaks the yoke of bondage and limitation. Using Moses and his rod as the primary metaphor—where the rod represents God's anointing—he demonstrates through scripture that believers must rely on the Holy Spirit's power rather than their own strength or strategies to accomplish God's work and experience miraculous deliverance and ministry growth.

lcOmEBXw6rw.title: 6 The Anointing in the Field - Joshua Heward-Mills
lcOmEBXw6rw.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills speaking; references his father Bishop Dag Heward-Mills' messages and ministry
lcOmEBXw6rw.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that discovering the anointing (God's presence in a person) requires recognizing and 'buying the field' (fully investing in that anointed vessel). He outlines the practical prices of obtaining anointing: time spent alone with God, openness/vulnerability, learning new things, adjustment to different styles, humility before greater authorities, listening/hearing, and honoring the anointed through giving and support. The sermon emphasizes that the anointing is not magic but requires intentional sacrifice and investment in studying messages, resources, and conferences to understand how God moves through anointed leaders.

eW5ahuc7QaA.title: 7 Receive the Anointed According to His True Rank - Joshua Heward-Mills
eW5ahuc7QaA.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that there are hierarchical rankings in how people receive and recognize the anointing—from receiving someone as God Himself, to as a prophet, to as a righteous man, to as a disciple—and that one's reward depends on the level at which they truly receive the anointed person. He emphasizes that discerning the true rank of an anointed minister based on spiritual fruit, evidence of God's presence, and ministry impact is essential for believers to access the full blessing and anointing that person carries. The sermon calls believers to stop claiming all ministers are equal, but rather to recognize and honor the distinctive mantles and grace God has placed on different servants according to their true spiritual rank and fruitfulness.

1YnYo2OYr9o.title: 8 Walk in the Blessedness of Transference of Spirits - Joshua Heward-Mills
1YnYo2OYr9o.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching on the transference of spirits and church growth anointing
1YnYo2OYr9o.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches on the blessedness of the transference of spirits, emphasizing that church growth anointing and ministerial capacity cannot be forced or learned but must be divinely transferred from one person to another. Using the example of Moses and the 70 elders, he explains that God takes the spirit resting on one leader and places it upon others to enable them to carry the burden of ministry and reach multitudes. The sermon highlights that receiving spiritual transference requires vigilance, openness, humility, and paying the price of time and dedication to position oneself to be a vessel for God's transfer.

vtrafi7f96o.title: 9 The Mystery of Impartation - Joshua Heward-Mills
vtrafi7f96o.preacherNote: Video title confirms Joshua Heward-Mills; auto-captions transcribed from live sermon on the anointing series
topics: The Role of Hearing in Spiritual Growth
vtrafi7f96o.keyScripture: Romans 1:10-11 (Paul's desire to travel to Rome to impart spiritual grace)
vtrafi7f96o.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches on the mystery of impartation—how the anointing is transferred from person to person through hearing, seeing, and physical presence rather than through written word or digital media alone. Using Paul's letter to the Romans as the primary text, he emphasizes that while Scripture is powerful, true spiritual impartation requires hearing the anointed voice, witnessing miracles and manifestations, and direct physical contact (laying on of hands). The sermon argues that proximity to anointed people and seeing the supernatural operate is essential for deep spiritual transfer and cannot be adequately replaced by podcasts or downloads.

1kYFh13-sIs.title: 10 The Spices of the Anointing - Joshua Heward-Mills
1kYFh13-sIs.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills speaking; title confirms identity
1kYFh13-sIs.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that every anointing has unique 'spices'—distinctive characteristics that flavor and distinguish one minister's calling from another, just as God prescribed specific spices for the tabernacle anointing oil. He illustrates how various ministries (prophetic, pastoral, evangelistic, apostolic) are enhanced by their unique spiritual seasoning—peculiar gifts, worship styles, healing manifestations, or teaching emphases that make their ministry distinctive and effective. He emphasizes that recognizing and embracing one's personal ministerial spice, rather than imitating others, is essential for authentic and powerful ministry.

IZQriFwoibo.title: 11 How You Can Change Your Anointing Levels - Joshua Heward-Mills
IZQriFwoibo.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at a camp session on changing anointing levels.
topics: Conviction as prerequisite for spiritual growth
IZQriFwoibo.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches how believers can change their anointing levels through three key steps: developing deep conviction that the anointing is essential, actively seeking knowledge by asking questions of spiritually mature leaders, and repenting with a genuine change of mind regarding priorities and worldly attachments. He emphasizes that the anointing is transferred from vessel to vessel through direct contact and investment of time, and that most believers fail to progress because they lack true conviction about what they really need spiritually.

5wwv2lgV2dg.title: The Key Of Watching And Praying | Joshua Heward-Mills
5wwv2lgV2dg.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at First Love Church
5wwv2lgV2dg.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that watching and praying is the foundational key to Christian discipleship, illustrated by Jesus Christ's example of rising early before dawn to pray in solitary places. The sermon emphasizes that prayer provides divine strength to overcome temptation, escape worldly snares, and maintain spiritual protection in daily life. Through contrasting the battles between flesh and spirit, Heward-Mills exhorts believers to build their spiritual strength through consistent morning prayer practice, warning against Christian complacency and shocks that come from losing connection with God's presence.

58Khj3668XA.title: 100%  Answered Prayers - Joshua Heward-Mills
58Khj3668XA.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at what appears to be a church service in Ghana, with announcements about an upcoming First Love camp (Feb 15-19) and teaching from his father Bishop Dag Heward-Mills' book 'How to Pray'
topics: The name of Jesus in prayer
58Khj3668XA.keyScripture: John 16:23 - "Ask the Father in my name and he will give it to you"
58Khj3668XA.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches on achieving 100% answered prayers by establishing proper prayer practices: praying directly to God the Father in the name of Jesus, confessing sins, and developing personal faith in God's promises rather than relying on intercessory prayer from others. He emphasizes that God's word is dependable and that confident prayer based on scriptural promises, exemplified by the prophet Elijah, will result in divine answers without exception.

94rOtjl7rrc.title: Prayer Creates New Dimensions in Ministry - Joshua Heward-Mills
topics: Access to new levels of blessing
94rOtjl7rrc.summary: Prayer possesses creative potential and gives birth to new dimensions in Christian life that cannot be accessed through natural means, education, or connections. Just as Zion travailed in prayer and brought forth children suddenly, believers can create new dimensions of holiness, purity, ministry growth, and breakthrough through intercession. The wisdom of God is mysterious—the causal link between prayer and answered blessings cannot be traced—yet prayer remains the only means of spiritual mobility and access to impossible dimensions of blessing.

SjN_1ebXaCs.title: The Key of Watching and Praying - Joshua Heward-Mills
SjN_1ebXaCs.note: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at Prophetic Encounter Service at First Love Centre

UErFSur--tg.title: How to Watch and Pray - Joshua Heward-Mills
UErFSur--tg.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at his church service
UErFSur--tg.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches on how to watch and pray, emphasizing the four night watches and the necessity of combining God's Word with prayer to sustain effective intercession. He explains that prayer without the Word becomes dry and unsustainable, and demonstrates three ways the Word empowers prayer: through Bible reading, through listening to preaching, and through studying books. The practical goal is for believers to experience the 'marrow' of God's presence during committed night prayer sessions rather than remaining at surface-level Christianity.

mNSY0o6cs1Y.title: The Qualities Needed for Watching and Praying - Joshua Heward-Mills
mNSY0o6cs1Y.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching final sermon of prayer series before joining crusade field
mNSY0o6cs1Y.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches the four essential qualities needed for watching and praying: diligence (persistent effort to seek God despite difficulty), persistence (continuing to search for God like hide-and-seek until found), faith (believing that God exists with His attributes and rewards those who seek Him), and boldness/impudence (confident declarations and commands made to God). He emphasizes that those who wait upon the Lord shall renew their strength, mount up with wings like eagles, run and not be weary, and walk and not faint—demonstrating that prayer is the pathway to spiritual power and recovery of lost purpose.

yHl1AdKvdGs.title: Quiet Time : The Secret of Many Great Men | Joshua Heward Mills
yHl1AdKvdGs.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills (BJosh) preaching at a church in the Philippines; his wife also briefly addresses the congregation
topics: Daily prayer and communion with the Lord
Moses as example of solitude with God
Knowing God's ways versus God's acts
Personal transformation through intimacy with God
Breaking free from sin through fellowship with Christ
yHl1AdKvdGs.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that spending daily quiet time alone with God is the secret that separated great biblical figures like Moses, Adam, and Prophet Micaiah from others, producing transformation and alignment with God's character and will. He emphasizes that no amount of church attendance, praise and worship participation, or pastoral relationships can replace personal intimate communion with the Holy Spirit. Through scriptural examples and personal anecdotes, he calls believers to prioritize daily alone time with God as the foundational discipline for spiritual growth, overcoming sin, and fulfilling destiny.

Y6fFq9Hq-6A.title: Quiet Time : The Most Important Relationship of Your Life | Joshua Heward Mills
Y6fFq9Hq-6A.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching on quiet time and personal relationship with God
Y6fFq9Hq-6A.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills emphasizes that daily quiet time alone with God is the most important relationship in life and the foundation for spiritual transformation. He illustrates how knowing God intimately through the secret place provides all spiritual provision, changes one's perspective on material desires, and produces a tangible divine presence that others can perceive. The sermon challenges believers to prioritize personal communion with God through prayer and Scripture reading over church attendance alone, arguing that this relationship supersedes all other relationships and accomplishments.

0ZBpvl4O_No.title: Quiet Time : The Most Important Habit of Your Life | Joshua Heward Mills
0ZBpvl4O_No.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at a conference service
0ZBpvl4O_No.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills emphasizes that developing a daily quiet time with God is the most important personal habit one can cultivate, not merely an occasional event. He teaches that consistent, unbroken fellowship with God through prayer and Bible reading prevents sin's dominance, produces spiritual fruit, and provides divine guidance for all areas of life. The sermon calls believers to establish a disciplined daily practice of seeking God's presence and meditating on Scripture, using Jesus' own example of rising early to pray in solitude.

FozYiEA60jE.title: Quiet Time : Draw Nigh to God | Joshua Heward-Mills
FozYiEA60jE.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at a conference (final day), addressing the importance of quiet time and drawing near to God.
FozYiEA60jE.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that drawing near to God through consistent quiet time is essential for Christian growth and transformation. When believers take steps toward God, He promises to draw near to them, resulting in cleansing from sin, hearing His voice, experiencing His goodness, and understanding His truth. God desires intimate relationship with His people and promises strength, dignity, prosperity, and preeminence to those who prioritize closeness with Him.

ozvAJMQ5Nek.title: Quiet Time : Sitting At The Feet Of The Holy Spirit | Joshua Heward-Mills
ozvAJMQ5Nek.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at COP (Church of Pentecost) conference closing session
ozvAJMQ5Nek.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that Christians must develop a daily quiet time alone with God, following Jesus's example of rising early to pray in solitude. He emphasizes that the Holy Spirit functions as a supernatural teacher who gradually reveals God's word and guidance to believers, addressing issues the sermon cannot cover. Spending intimate time with God's presence provides complete satisfaction and spiritual transformation that cannot be achieved through busy service alone.

N6yh62_otOs.title: Hear The Words From His Mouth | Meeting God | Joshua Heward-Mills
N6yh62_otOs.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches the critical distinction between logos (written word of God) and rhema (spoken, living word of God), using Mary sitting at Jesus's feet as the model for receiving God's specific word. He emphasizes that faith comes through hearing the rhema, not merely reading scripture, and that spiritual transformation requires intimate time waiting for God to make His written word alive and personal in one's heart. The sermon illustrates how one must receive a specific word from God for a specific person at a specific time to walk in true faith and God's will.

HuOIRD7ocIw.title: Sitting At Jesus’ Feet | Meeting God | Joshua Heward-Mills
HuOIRD7ocIw.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at his church in Ghana
topics: Closeness versus distance in relationship with God
HuOIRD7ocIw.keyScripture: Luke 10:39 - "She had a sister called Mary, which also sat at Jesus' feet and heard his word."
HuOIRD7ocIw.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches on the importance of drawing near to God through intimacy and solitude, using Mary and Martha as the central example. He explains that the "one thing needful" is sitting at Jesus' feet—being physically and spiritually close to God rather than merely working for Him from a distance. The sermon emphasizes seven blessings of drawing near to God, including His drawing near to you, cleansing of hands and purification of hearts, and the ability to hear His voice clearly.

F3IFXXenNto.title: The Intimacy That Changes Us | Meeting God with Joshua Heward-Mills
F3IFXXenNto.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at what appears to be a church service with worship, offering collection, and a sermon on spiritual transformation.
topics: Gifts of the Holy Spirit vs fruits of the Holy Spirit
The Holy Spirit's role in true change
The difference between real and fake transformation
F3IFXXenNto.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches on three types of spiritual change described in scripture: metamorphosis (complete transformation by the Spirit), metaschematizo (false outward change), and alaso (future glorification). He emphasizes that true change comes only through the Holy Spirit's work, not through human willpower or self-effort, and distinguishes between the gifts of the Holy Spirit (which can coexist in wicked people) and the fruits of the Holy Spirit (which reflect genuine character transformation). The sermon challenges believers to seek genuine intimacy with God that produces real internal change rather than mere external religious conformity.

DKM-j7rpwg4.title: Living In The Shadow Of God | Meeting God with Joshua Heward-Mills
DKM-j7rpwg4.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at First Love Center
topics: Living in the shadow of God
Secret place of the Most High
Arrangement and commitment to meeting God
Sharpening and polishing in God's presence
DKM-j7rpwg4.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that living under God's shadow means dwelling constantly in His presence, not merely visiting occasionally through prayer or crisis. True dwelling requires deliberate life arrangement and commitment to consistently return to the secret place, resulting in continuous access to God's wisdom, joy, liberty, and provision. This habitual presence transforms believers into vessels of God's power, sharpening their effectiveness in all areas of life.

jJcYILHaHbk.title: Jesus And The Secret Place | Meeting God with Joshua Heward-Mills
topics: Private vs public encounters with God
Life of Christ as light and guidance
jJcYILHaHbk.summary: Jesus demonstrated the priority of meeting God alone in the secret place by rising early in the morning before dawn to pray in a solitary location, establishing a pattern that believers should follow. The secret place is characterized by solitude, darkness, and separation from distractions, where one's life is shaped by intimate encounters with God's word and presence rather than public religious displays. Private devotion to God through prayer and study of Scripture is essential for spiritual power and authentic Christian living, as exemplified by Christ's life and the lives of biblical figures like Moses, Elijah, and Samuel.

ZbJV1wP-h74.title: One Thing Is Needful | London I Joshua Heward-Mills
scriptures: Matthew 4 (Man shall not live by bread alone)
Ephesians 5:26 (Washing of water by the word)
ZbJV1wP-h74.summary: Jesus taught that only one thing is needful—being at His feet and hearing His word—contrasting Martha's anxious busyness with Mary's focus on God's presence. Bishop Heward-Mills emphasizes that Satan deceives us into believing we need other things (approval, relationships, possessions) to be fulfilled, when only intimacy with Jesus and hearing His rhema (specific word from God) truly satisfies. The Christian life is not about service, position, or human validation, but about proximity to God and receiving His personal word that brings faith, healing, and direction.

E597t0NQwkI.title: Ministering to the Lord  | Joshua Heward-Mills
E597t0NQwkI.preacherNote: Identified by video title and sermon content on BJosh channel
E597t0NQwkI.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches believers how to minister to the Lord through worship and singing, emphasizing that God created us for His glory and desire for our praise. True worship requires both spirit and truth, and can be practiced privately through sincere singing and prayer regardless of location or circumstances. The speaker stresses that authentic intimacy with God's presence comes from deep personal devotion rather than formal settings or external activities.

2ebOBrveMgc.title: God Has Friends | Knowing God Part 1 | Joshua Heward-Mills | First Love Center
2ebOBrveMgc.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at First Love Center
topics: Signs of new life in Christ
2ebOBrveMgc.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that salvation births supernatural spiritual desires—for God's presence, the Word, souls, spiritual gifts, and the local church—that distinguish a living spirit from a dead one. He emphasizes that Christians must cultivate the supreme desire for God Himself as their ultimate satisfaction and portion, understanding that contentment in Him is greater than pursuing earthly possessions or relationships. The core message is that daily proximity to God through prayer and devotion, rather than external circumstances, brings the lasting joy and peace that Jesus demonstrated even unto the cross.

qjmpxDQN1Cg.title: Those Who Don’t Know God | Knowing God Part 2 | Joshua Heward-Mills
qjmpxDQN1Cg.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching part 2 of 'Knowing God' series, inspired by 'The Sweet Influence of the Holy Spirit' chapter 3
topics: Purpose of creation - relationship with God
The problem of sin and separation from God
Jesus Christ's death and the torn veil
Eternal life defined as knowing God
qjmpxDQN1Cg.summary: God created humanity in His image specifically to have relationship and conversation with Him, not merely for worship or obedience. Sin created separation between God and man, symbolized by the veil in the holy of holies that only the high priest could enter once yearly. Jesus Christ's death tore that veil, removing the barrier and restoring the opportunity for all believers to know God intimately as friends rather than servants, which is the true meaning of eternal life.

t3eaNOMx44A.title: Can I Know God? | Knowing God Part 3 | Joshua Heward-Mills
t3eaNOMx44A.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching 'Knowing God Part 3: Can I Know God?'
t3eaNOMx44A.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills explores why many Christians struggle to believe they can personally know God, identifying mental barriers including God's greatness, invisibility, inability to be heard, and inaccessibility. He contrasts human relationships with divine friendship, explaining how parameters like wealth, wisdom, power, and influence create psychological distance. The sermon emphasizes that God desires intimate friendship with all people and presents prophetic evidence that knowing God personally is biblically promised and achievable despite these perceived obstacles.

OqJ-BrLZVeI.title: The Holy Spirit, The God You Can Befriend | Knowing God Part 4 | Joshua Heward-Mills
OqJ-BrLZVeI.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaching on friendship with God and the role of the Holy Spirit
OqJ-BrLZVeI.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that the Holy Spirit is God's gift of intimate friendship, distinguishing this from the Father's love and the Son's grace. He explains that while the Father cannot be a friend (residing in heaven), and Jesus could not be physically present everywhere, the Holy Spirit provides constant companionship and the means through which believers truly know God. Knowing God—not merely believing in Him—is the highest calling and is accomplished exclusively through intimate fellowship with the Holy Spirit.

wsIuuqgPU8w.title: How the Holy Spirit Helps You to Know God | Knowing God Part 5 | Joshua Heward-Mills
wsIuuqgPU8w.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching on Part 5 of 'Knowing God' series
wsIuuqgPU8w.summary: The Holy Spirit helps you know God by first guiding you into all truth—requiring genuine belief in God's word, not merely mental assent. The Spirit also reminds you of God's promises and past faithfulness when you forget them, bringing scripture to remembrance in moments when you need it most. Without the work of the Holy Spirit, humans struggle with spiritual forgetfulness and cannot develop a true relationship with God, requiring divine enablement to genuinely believe and remember what He has spoken.

wDOGB5xqLf4.title: How the Holy Spirit Helps You to Know God Part 2 | Knowing God Part 6 | Joshua Heward-Mills
wDOGB5xqLf4.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching Part 2 of 'How the Holy Spirit Helps You to Know God' series
wDOGB5xqLf4.summary: The Holy Spirit gives believers spiritual knowings or unction that enables them to discern truth from deception, good influences from bad, and helps protect their relationship with God. Third-party influences—whether false friends, antichrist figures, or worldly connections—are the greatest enemies of intimacy with God, as demonstrated from Adam and Eve's fall when a third party (Satan) was introduced into their relationship with the Creator. The Holy Spirit indwells believers to keep them in God's presence and helps them recognize and avoid relationships and influences that would lead them away from knowing and following God.

KgVTRipVI94.title: The Blessings of Knowing God | Knowing God Part 7 | Joshua Heward-Mills
KgVTRipVI94.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching part 7 of 'Knowing God' series
KgVTRipVI94.summary: Those who know God will be strong and do exploits, while those who don't know Him remain weak. True strength comes from a personal relationship with God, not from church attendance, worship style, or the preacher's reputation.
