The blessings of knowing God include being built up, experiencing signs and wonders, having peace and rest, and being selected for ministry work that only God's friends can accomplish.

C146E1ltAm8.title: What Should I Do Tomorrow Morning? | Meeting God | Joshua Heward-Mills
C146E1ltAm8.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching on knowing God and fellowship with the Holy Spirit
topics: Seeking God in darkness and uncertainty
C146E1ltAm8.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that beginning tomorrow morning, believers should develop deep fellowship (koinonia) with the Holy Spirit through direct communication and recognizing Him as the sole distributor of all God's gifts and blessings. The Holy Spirit communicates with us just as we communicate with others, and this relationship is not based on formulas or steps but on willingness to seek God through uncertainty, just as Abraham and other biblical figures did. Without active fellowship with the Holy Spirit—asking Him for wisdom, guidance, healing, and every spiritual gift—believers remain saved but spiritually impoverished, unable to access what God has already provided through Him.

0x3LiOC5lm0.title: Tell Them Pt  2 The Love Of Jesus Christ - Joshua Heward-Mills
0x3LiOC5lm0.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching; opens with greetings from his father Bishop Dag Heward-Mills in Zambia
0x3LiOC5lm0.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills preaches the second part of his "Tell Them" series on the love of Jesus Christ, establishing that Christ's love is the greatest love of all time. He systematically compares Jesus's sacrificial love against five worldly loves—brotherly love, national love, maternal love, romantic love, and spousal love—demonstrating that none can match Christ's unconditional, redemptive love for sinners. The sermon emphasizes that Jesus's love stands alone because He sacrificed everything while we were yet sinners, making Him worthy of worship and motivating believers toward soul-winning.

90l0U1zgZdw.title: Life, Death and Judgement - Joshua Heward-Mills
90l0U1zgZdw.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaches on life, death, and judgment using John 3:16 as the foundation, explaining God's miraculous love for the world and His provision of salvation through Christ's blood. He describes life as a university with compulsory subjects (death and judgment) and an elective choice (whom to serve and whether to live for God). The sermon concludes with an altar call inviting listeners to rededicate their lives to Jesus and prepare for inevitable judgment by accepting salvation today.

9el1SQrdFmM.title: Not Everyone Who Calls Me Lord Lord Shall Enter the Kingdom  - Joshua Heward-Mills
9el1SQrdFmM.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at The Great Invitation Gospel Encounter Service
9el1SQrdFmM.summary: Jesus prophesies that many who call Him 'Lord Lord' will not enter the kingdom of heaven because they lack true heart relationship with God—merely external religious practice and lip service cannot save. True Christianity requires complete submission of body, mind, and spirit to Jesus as Master and Lord, not just verbal confession or religious works. God looks at the heart and requires genuine transformation (being born again) and obedience, not hollow religious performance.

J_lQARe4P8Q.title: Why Philip Preached Christ   YOF  - Joshua Heward-Mills
J_lQARe4P8Q.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at a conference; brings greetings from his father Bishop Douglas Heward-Mills and the First Love Church.
J_lQARe4P8Q.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills explains why Philip preached Christ to the Samaritans: because Christ is the only hope of glory and the resurrection and the life that matters when standing before God. He challenges Christians that the unchanging purpose of Christianity is evangelism and soul-winning, not earthly prosperity or worldly blessings, and calls believers to abandon competing topics and commit to preaching Christ crucified as the sole means of salvation.

PlHOHg4uIPg.title: The Prodigal Son - Joshua Heward-Mills
PlHOHg4uIPg.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at UPSA (University of Professional Studies, Accra)
PlHOHg4uIPg.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills expounds the parable of the Prodigal Son to illustrate how rebellion against God and departure from His house leads to emptiness, while returning to God and recognizing Him as Father brings restoration. He challenges the audience (primarily university students) to recognize their own spiritual rebellion, worldly pursuits, and sexual immorality as evidence they have lost their way from God. The sermon emphasizes that true love, fulfillment, and wisdom come only through submitting to God as Shepherd and having one's sins washed by the blood of Jesus Christ.

VvTY-dX7t0E.title: Salvation Through The Blood of Jesus  - Joshua Heward-Mills
VvTY-dX7t0E.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaching on salvation and the redemptive power of Christ's blood.
topics: Power of the blood of Jesus
VvTY-dX7t0E.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaches on salvation exclusively through the blood of Jesus Christ, emphasizing that salvation is by grace through faith, not by works. He explains multiple judgments believers face and distinguishes between salvation judgment (having the Son of God) and works judgment (bearing fruit). The message centers on the transformative and redemptive power of Christ's blood to forgive sins, reconcile sinners to God, and cleanse believers, culminating in an altar call for those who want to surrender their lives to Jesus and receive forgiveness.

f_SSeowDc-w.title: Dont Be Surprised That You Must Be Born Again - Joshua Heward-Mills
f_SSeowDc-w.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills preaches that being born again requires genuine acknowledgment of personal sin and wrongdoing rather than focusing on external righteousness or comparing oneself to others. He emphasizes that pride and refusal to admit fault prevent people from coming to God, using the Pharisee and Publican parable to contrast self-justification with sincere repentance. The message calls listeners to confess their sins, accept God's correction, and surrender their lives completely to Christ for genuine transformation.

gb0t0zomg5w.title: Almost - Joshua Heward-Mills
gb0t0zomg5w.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at Legon University
topics: Peer pressure and fear of man
gb0t0zomg5w.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills uses the biblical account of King Agrippa's response to Paul's gospel preaching to challenge listeners against remaining 'almost' persuaded to become Christians. He emphasizes that nearness to salvation without commitment—due to peer pressure, worldly attachments, or fear of man—results in spiritual death, using his own testimony of conversion at age 19 to illustrate genuine surrender to Christ. The sermon calls for immediate, public commitment to Jesus rather than postponement, warning that 'almost' is never sufficient for salvation.

gjY6UcPwTQg.title: The Gospel In A Nutshell - Joshua Heward-Mills
gjY6UcPwTQg.preacherNote: Pastor Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at Salvation Rally 2018
gjY6UcPwTQg.summary: Pastor Joshua Heward-Mills explains the Gospel in its simplest and most essential form through John 3:16, emphasizing God's incomparable love for the world and His offer of salvation through belief in Christ to all people regardless of background or sin. He contrasts God's infinite greatness—as Creator of all things and source of all true power, wealth, and authority—with His universal and unconditional love that accepts 'whosoever believeth.' The sermon urgently calls listeners to accept Jesus Christ as Savior through faith alone, warning of judgment and hell while assuring that salvation requires no qualifications except belief and surrender to Christ.

i6EmjEz8kOw.title: The Supernatural Abilities of The Blood  - Joshua Heward-Mills
i6EmjEz8kOw.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaches the main sermon after testimonies and worship.
i6EmjEz8kOw.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches on the three supernatural abilities of the blood of Jesus: (1) the blood never expires, unlike human blood which expires after 42 days, making Jesus's blood eternally powerful because it contains the Holy Ghost; (2) the blood saves from all deserved punishments and completely washes away sins in a way that teaching, singing, or good works cannot; (3) the blood has the supernatural ability to prevent death because life itself is in the blood, making it the only means of atonement and salvation.

if-iGhdBvIg.title: There Is A Way That Seemeth Right Unto Men  - Joshua Heward-Mills
if-iGhdBvIg.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at a Supernatural Salvation Rally
topics: There is a way that seemeth right unto men
The end of unwise paths leads to death
Wealth cannot save in the day of wrath
The name of Jesus as strong tower
Salvation and the cost of following Christ
Secret sins and humility before God
Money and material pursuits as false security
The importance of submission to God's plan
if-iGhdBvIg.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills preaches on Proverbs 14:12, explaining how people pursue worldly paths—wealth, pleasure, and material success—believing these will bring happiness and security, when in truth these ways lead to death and destruction. He uses personal stories and biblical examples to show that riches cannot save in the day of God's wrath, and that only by running into the strong tower of God's name through Jesus Christ can one find true salvation and protection. The sermon calls listeners to abandon their own plans and submit to God's superior plan, which alone leads to eternal life and genuine fulfillment.

o-61QpA3_pE.title: By His Stripes  - Joshua Heward-Mills
o-61QpA3_pE.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at the first location where he ever preached, approximately 10 years prior. He mentions not feeling well but emphasizes this is a night of miracles and healing.
o-61QpA3_pE.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills preaches on Isaiah 53, focusing on Christ's suffering and redemption through His stripes. He emphasizes that belief in this prophetic report activates God's power for healing and miracles in the listener's life. The sermon highlights that Christ bore our sicknesses, sorrows, sins, and iniquities, making supernatural wholeness available to those who believe.

wtAY4yfU1Go.title: Remember Lots' Wife  - Joshua Heward-Mills
wtAY4yfU1Go.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills (BJosh) preaching at First Love Center
wtAY4yfU1Go.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches on the danger of divided commitment to God, using the story of Lot's wife as a central warning against looking back at the world while claiming to follow Christ. He emphasizes that true service to God requires complete surrender and abandonment of worldly pursuits, illustrated through testimonies of young people who received supernatural provision when they fully committed to God's work. The sermon culminates in an altar call urging listeners to completely renounce their old lives and fully commit their hearts to Jesus without reservation.

y-Pp4wfdBZk.title: Without  - Joshua Heward-Mills
y-Pp4wfdBZk.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at a salvation rally/conference
topics: Being set apart from the world
y-Pp4wfdBZk.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that believers cannot live without four essential pillars: Jesus Christ, Holiness, Faith, and (implied fourth point). He emphasizes that while material possessions and modern conveniences are dispensable, these spiritual foundations are non-negotiable for salvation, pleasing God, and eternal life. The sermon challenges Christians to be peculiar and separate from worldly systems, demonstrating genuine faith through works rather than mere words.

eriMY5ZHx9A.title: I Have Missed You - Joshua Heward-Mills
eriMY5ZHx9A.preacherNote: Video title confirms 'Joshua Heward-Mills'; speaker identifies himself as BJosh and references personal ministry experience throughout.
topics: God's presence over works and achievements
Relationship with God versus religious performance
Leadership and spiritual authority through God's presence
Morning prayer and communion with God
Being close to God as primary calling
eriMY5ZHx9A.keyScripture: Daniel 11:32 - Those who know their God shall be strong and do exploits
eriMY5ZHx9A.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills emphasizes that knowing God personally is the foundation of all ministry and blessing, far more important than external achievements, large congregations, or material success. He illustrates this through the examples of Abraham, Moses, and the early disciples, teaching that intimate prayer and communion with God's presence produces both spiritual power and practical fruit in ministry. The central message is that God desires relationship with His servants first—expressed in the refrain 'I have missed you'—and that this intimacy becomes the source of all true anointing and effective ministry.

KUFcUIXWO9o.title: Honour Your Prophet - Joshua Heward-Mills
KUFcUIXWO9o.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching; his father Bishop Dag Heward-Mills mentioned as absent (in Manila)
topics: The role of prophets in church
KUFcUIXWO9o.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that honouring prophets and the anointing is essential for receiving God's miraculous power, using Mark 6 to show how Jesus could do no mighty works where He was not honoured. He emphasizes that receiving a prophet in the name of a prophet brings a prophet's reward, and that spiritual authority and God's presence on a leader must be valued through proper honour and substantial offerings. The sermon stresses that many Christians fail to recognize and respect the anointing, treating spiritual leaders merely as men rather than vessels of the Holy Spirit, which limits God's power in their lives.

Pp1v0JN5qfc.title: First Works  - Joshua Heward-Mills
Pp1v0JN5qfc.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at University Christian Fellowship (UCF), Legon campus
topics: Power of the Holy Spirit for witnessing
End times and urgency of conversion
True love through action not feeling
Pp1v0JN5qfc.keyScripture: Revelation 2:4-5 (Remember first works; repent or candlestick removed)
Pp1v0JN5qfc.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills challenges the church—especially campus Christian leaders—to return to first works of soul-winning and gospel preaching rather than pursuing worldly desires, false prosperity gospels, and performative worship. He emphasizes that true love of Christ is demonstrated through action (witnessing and conversion), not feelings, and warns that churches abandoning the primary mission of reaching the lost will be removed from their place of influence. The sermon culminates in prayer for the release of Holy Spirit power upon believers to become witnesses and soul-winners before Christ's return.

32yKeo4vbJE.title: By His Grace - Joshua Heward-Mills
32yKeo4vbJE.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that grace is a spirit—a supernatural ease and ability—that God pours upon His people to accomplish His purposes without struggle or human effort. He emphasizes that grace appears to everyone through the gospel, but must be recognized and received to manifest in one's life, using examples of natural talents, business success, and church growth. The sermon concludes with prayer for the congregation to receive a transferable grace from the life of the prophet that will transform their circumstances from difficulty to ease.

5E4RQVeykiE.title: Mega Church pt 1 - Joshua Heward-Mills
5E4RQVeykiE.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching on mega church benefits and manifestations of the Holy Spirit
5E4RQVeykiE.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches why believers should be part of a mega church, emphasizing that expectation in corporate gatherings releases the power of God's manifestations. He explains that the Holy Spirit operates with greater might when multiple hearts of faith gather together, citing the law of multiple administrations where God repeatedly ministers to believers throughout their lives. The sermon calls believers to expect miracles, healings, and supernatural gifts of the Spirit while warning against sin and concluding with an altar call for salvation.

Al69U15zbe8.title: The Assembling of the Saints - Joshua Heward-Mills
Al69U15zbe8.preacherNote: Pastor Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at his church; includes worship, testimonies, and sermon.
topics: Presence of God in corporate worship
Mount Zion and the Holy Jerusalem
Al69U15zbe8.summary: Pastor Joshua emphasizes the urgency of Jesus's imminent return through multiple biblical signs (wars, famines, famines, persecution, false prophets). He teaches that the primary Christian response to these end times is faithful assembly with the Saints in corporate worship, where God's presence is most powerfully manifested and believers are protected from Satan's deceptions. The assembly silences the voice of the Accuser and transforms believers through the power of God's Word in corporate settings.

x_27D9ZNKg4.title: The Assembling of Saints Pt  2
x_27D9ZNKg4.preacherNote: Pastor Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at First Love Church during 'The Assembling of Saints Pt 2' service
x_27D9ZNKg4.summary: Pastor Joshua teaches that assembling together as believers provides four critical blessings: the presence of God, protection from Satan's deception, spiritual power to deal with serious sins, and provision of family and material needs. He emphasizes that Satan is uncomfortable and ineffective in the corporate assembly, using illustrations of how the atmosphere of unified worship (prayer, worship, singing) defeats demonic activity. The message concludes with teaching on Jehovah Jireh (God will see and provide), using Abraham's ram provision to show that God supplies needs when we gather in faith at His house.

8EAZ8LTLS3s.title: The Assembling Of Saints Pt 3 - Joshua Heward-Mills
8EAZ8LTLS3s.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaches the main message after worship, testimonies, and a video appearance by his father Bishop Dag Heward-Mills.
8EAZ8LTLS3s.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills expounds on 1 Corinthians 14, explaining that preaching serves three purposes: edification (building up the spirit), exhortation (encouraging troops in spiritual warfare), and comfort. He illustrates how regular church attendance constructs one's spirit through the oikodomeo (Greek: building/edification), enabling believers to overcome fleshly temptations through spiritual strength. He shares personal testimonies of resisting temptation and emphasizes that spiritual construction is continuous, comparing it to the patience required in physical building projects.

8_fU9uUzOx4.title: The Prevailing Word Of God - Joshua Heward-Mills
8_fU9uUzOx4.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at City Church
8_fU9uUzOx4.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills emphasizes that God's Word grows and prevails in the lives of believers, using the parable of the mustard seed to illustrate how Scripture, though appearing small and insignificant compared to life's problems, contains miraculous power that transforms circumstances over time. He challenges listeners to actively sow God's Word into their hearts through daily Bible study and quiet time, warning that spiritual victory comes not through pastors, shouts, or external circumstances, but exclusively through the living, God-breathed Word that accomplishes what God sends it to do. The sermon calls believers away from superficial Christianity toward a deeper commitment to studying and meditating on Scripture as the foundation of a genuine relationship with God.

DgW1UAmXmWQ.title: Be Ready For A Long Fight - Joshua Heward-Mills
topics: Prophecy as a call to action not automatic blessing
Armor of God and spiritual defense
DgW1UAmXmWQ.summary: Bishop Joshua emphasizes that receiving a prophecy or promise from God is not automatic fulfillment but rather a declaration of what God has given you to fight for and claim through sustained effort and perseverance. He teaches that spiritual growth requires battle in four dimensions: personal (overcoming sin and attitude), family (navigating relational conflicts), leadership (influencing others toward righteousness), and public (maintaining witness). The message culminates in a prophetic word for 2018, encouraging believers that regardless of past failures and setbacks, God remains faithful to complete His work in their lives if they continue standing and fighting the good fight of faith.

Ks0LkSqgLBc.title: Bebelos - Joshua Heward-Mills
Ks0LkSqgLBc.preacherNote: Pastor Joshua Heward-Mills preaching; Bishop Dag Heward-Mills (his father) away in South Africa
topics: The power of the name Jesus
Light and darkness in Christian life
Ruth as outsider becoming ancestor of David
Recognizing and valuing God's call and anointing
Sacred vs. profane attitude toward spiritual gifts
Testimony and faith in God's protection
scriptures: Proverbs (unspecified verse about wise servant ruling)
Ks0LkSqgLBc.summary: Pastor Joshua teaches on the Greek word 'bebelos' (profane/contemptuous), meaning those who despise holy and spiritual things, using Esau's disregard for his birthright as a primary example. He explains how despising what God has placed before us—including anointed leadership, spiritual gifts, and divine callings—causes us to miss blessings that then go to unlikely people, as illustrated by Ruth the Moabite becoming part of Jesus's lineage. The sermon emphasizes that through our actions and attitudes we can either honor or despise the anointing and call of God in our lives.

uaB_2PFVkdw.title: He That Hath (Chapter Four) - Joshua Heward-Mills
uaB_2PFVkdw.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at what appears to be a Saturday evening service
uaB_2PFVkdw.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills expounds on Jesus' principle that "he that hath, to him shall be given; and he that hath not, from him shall be taken even that which he hath." He explains that inequality and intractable poverty stem not from external circumstances but from three internal factors: lack of knowledge, lack of skill, and laziness. Through teaching, biblical examples (particularly Joseph), and the grace of God, believers can transform from "he that hath not" to "he that hath" by acquiring knowledge and developing spiritual and practical skills.

Wt7fh1FJM_k.title: Redeeming The Time - Joshua Heward-Mills
Wt7fh1FJM_k.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaches the main sermon after testimonies and worship
Wt7fh1FJM_k.summary: Pastor Joshua teaches that believers must 'redeem the time' by recognizing that life and opportunity are finite resources, using Ephesians 5:15-16 to emphasize walking circumspectly and wisely rather than as fools. He illustrates how time is relative to location, leadership, and spiritual season, and challenges the congregation to understand God's mandate for service, soul-winning, and fruitfulness while they still have the opportunity. The sermon urgently calls believers to maximize their time in God's work before the window of opportunity closes, using his own example of tireless service in outreach and preparation.

XOP5NJzrThk.title: Seeds and Harvest - Joshua Heward-Mills
XOP5NJzrThk.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at OSU Center, introduced by Pastor Frank whom he mentored years ago.
XOP5NJzrThk.summary: God's design for provision operates through the principle of sowing seeds—spiritual, material, and relational—with the guarantee that harvest follows according to scripture. Christians must understand that good things (food, kindness, honor, service, mercy) are seeds that produce corresponding blessings, and that without sowing, there is nothing for God to bless. The sermon emphasizes that blessing is not arbitrary but is the direct result of seeds sown in obedience, illustrated through personal testimonies and biblical examples including Abraham, the widow at Zarephath, and the principle that whatever a person sows, they will reap.

Yifgbqgm09s.title: Temple Blessings - Joshua Heward-Mills
Yifgbqgm09s.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at First Love Church
Yifgbqgm09s.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that believers must identify as servants of God who work actively in the church to receive temple blessings and divine rewards. He emphasizes that God's primary purpose is building His church, and those who faithfully serve receive wages from heaven—protection, provision, employment, and breakthroughs—while those who merely attend without serving miss these benefits. Three testimonies demonstrate how members received jobs, visas, money, and academic success by prioritizing church work and prayer despite other challenges.

agipC9XhVC8.title: Keep The Munition - Joshua Heward-Mills
agipC9XhVC8.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching; title confirms BJosh
agipC9XhVC8.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that Christians must be spiritually armed and prepared for spiritual warfare, not blindly expecting only blessings. Using Ephesians 6, he explains that the only offensive weapon is the sword of the Spirit—God's Word—and that deep knowledge of Scripture through regular quiet time is essential for transformation, protection, and victorious Christian living. Over the coming weeks, he will teach how the Word of God provides practical guidance for every area of life and is the key to knowing God intimately and standing firm when adversity comes.

caSy1ft6u24.title: How To Be A Humble Minister Like Jesus - Joshua Heward-Mills
caSy1ft6u24.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching; father Bishop Dag Heward-Mills mentioned as away in Manila
topics: Jesus as Son of God and Son of Man
caSy1ft6u24.keyScripture: Matthew 11:28-29 (Take my yoke upon you and learn from me, for I am meek and humble in heart)
caSy1ft6u24.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that Jesus Christ's greatest lesson for believers is humility, not miracles or power. He emphasizes that divine selection operates through the law of humility—God grants grace and breakthrough to the humble while resisting the proud, as illustrated by Jesus' interactions with the Syrophoenician woman and the centurion. The sermon challenges churchgoers to abandon pride and spiritual superiority, embracing meekness as the pathway to inheriting earthly blessings and receiving God's help in all circumstances.

hqZ89h88cQg.title: The Relationships of Spiritual People - Joshua Heward-Mills
hqZ89h88cQg.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at what appears to be a miracle service
topics: Knowing people in the spirit vs. the flesh
Spirituality vs. carnality in the church
Paul's authority on spiritual vs. carnal believers
The effect of the cross on relationships
Prophets and spiritual people as sources of authority
Doctrine and manner of life as marks of knowing someone spiritually
Persecutions and faith as evidence of spiritual knowledge
hqZ89h88cQg.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that spiritual people must relate to one another in the spirit rather than in the flesh, based on Paul's instruction that believers should no longer know anyone after the flesh but only spiritually. True spiritual relationship is determined by knowing a person's doctrine, manner of life, faith, purpose, and persecutions endured—not by family connections, nationality, age, or physical proximity. The cross of Christ fundamentally changed how believers should relate, requiring them to recognize and honor the anointing and spiritual reality in people rather than being distracted by carnal details.

qJP6res5n4c.title: In the Atmosphere of War - Joshua Heward-Mills
qJP6res5n4c.preacherNote: Pastor Joshua Heward-Mills preached while Bishop Dag Heward-Mills was absent
qJP6res5n4c.summary: Pastor Joshua teaches that Christianity is not a peaceful refuge but a spiritual war requiring constant fighting and perseverance. He challenges believers' romanticized view of faith, explaining that genuine Christian service demands dedication despite personal struggles, pain, and adversity. Through biblical examples and personal testimony, he urges the church to embrace the 'atmosphere of war' and continue their spiritual work—whether preaching, outreach, or worship—regardless of circumstances, completing their assigned course like Paul did.

qTas2ie1TMY.title: Fortify Thy Power Mightily - Joshua Heward-Mills
qTas2ie1TMY.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaching on Christian spiritual power and maturity after the new birth.
qTas2ie1TMY.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that being born again is only the first miracle; believers must subsequently be filled with and empowered by the Holy Spirit to live transformed Christian lives. Using the metaphor of an unbilical cord that must be cut, he challenges Christians to mature beyond spiritual dependence on pastors and churches, allowing the Holy Spirit to change their minds, behaviors, and spiritual strongholds. The sermon emphasizes that the Holy Spirit—not mere proximity to Christ or religious activity—is the essential agent of lasting transformation in the believer's life.

LvDcywoCkMg.title: The House Of God - Joshua Heward-Mills
LvDcywoCkMg.preacherNote: Video title confirms 'Joshua Heward-Mills'; sermon opens with worship songs then transitions to preaching at 'Matthew chapter 16 and verse 15'
LvDcywoCkMg.summary: Jesus declared 'I will build my church' (Matthew 16:18), establishing that God's primary purpose on earth is constructing His church, not worldly enterprises. Bishop Heward-Mills emphasizes that believers must align themselves with God's eternal building project by actively investing their time, resources, and talents into church growth and ministry rather than pursuing temporary material gains. Those who genuinely love and serve God's house will prosper according to Psalm 122:6, while those who neglect this calling experience emptiness and lack despite their own worldly pursuits.

sMVBnBaiskI.title: Why Are You Still Alive   Ready For the Masters Use Conference - Joshua Heward-Mills
sMVBnBaiskI.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at 'Ready For the Masters Use Conference'
sMVBnBaiskI.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills preaches on why Christians remain alive on earth despite heavenly promises, arguing that God calls many (not few) believers to purposeful service and fruitfulness rather than mere personal salvation. He emphasizes that believers are untied from sin's bondage for the Master's use—specifically to preach the gospel and win souls—not for comfort or prosperity. The sermon urges the younger generation to embrace their calling to evangelize and be witnesses in their cities, warning that the message of Christ's blood and salvation has been diluted by other emphases.

Oko_WnuHCws.title: The Master Key of Loving God - Joshua Heward-Mills
Oko_WnuHCws.preacherNote: Self-identified as Joshua Heward-Mills throughout; mentions his father Bishop Dag Heward-Mills sending love.
topics: Loving God as the master key
The three levels of love (heart, soul, mind)
Oko_WnuHCws.keyScripture: Matthew 22:37 - Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy mind
Oko_WnuHCws.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that loving God—with heart (belief), soul (emotion/feelings), and mind (calculation)—is the master key that unlocks the supernatural in a believer's life. He contrasts the natural man, who cannot receive God's blessings, with those who enter the spiritual realm through genuine love and desire for God's presence. This transformative love releases divine grace, protection, and answered prayers, affecting every area of life: family, prosperity, ministry, and deliverance from trouble.

n3jRbJ0W0jI.title: The Beginning of Miracles   GEM Chapel - Joshua Heward-Mills
n3jRbJ0W0jI.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at GEM Chapel
n3jRbJ0W0jI.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that recognizing God's small miracles—like Jesus turning water into wine at Cana—is essential for experiencing greater miracles. He emphasizes that grateful acknowledgment of God's sufficiency in provision and blessing opens the door to multiplication and greater manifestations of God's power. The congregation is challenged to count their blessings, write them down, give a thankful offering, and mark an 'Ebenezer' stone of gratitude for what God has already accomplished.

0UxRlq1ZcEI.title: The Good Samaritan and Others - Joshua Heward-Mills
0UxRlq1ZcEI.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at what appears to be a multi-campus church service (Main Campus, North Campus, East Campus, South Campus mentioned)
topics: Church's responsibility to care for others
0UxRlq1ZcEI.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills expounds the parable of the Good Samaritan as a prophetic revelation of Jesus Christ's sacrificial love and redemptive work, showing how Jesus—the despised outsider—rescued humanity from sin's consequences and calls the Church to similarly care for the lost. He emphasizes that Jesus's blood (the wine) and the Holy Spirit (the oil) are essential to salvation and transformation, with the Church's role being to minister to those Jesus brings in. The sermon culminates in an altar call emphasizing that only Jesus's blood can save sinners and transform lives, calling believers to embrace their purpose of carrying this message to a dying world.

0_YSAS_PdGc.title: Jesus and the Rich Fool - Joshua Heward-Mills
0_YSAS_PdGc.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at First Love Church
0_YSAS_PdGc.summary: Jesus teaches against covetousness through the parable of the rich fool, emphasizing that a person's life does not consist of material possessions and warning that those who live solely for earthly gain are spiritually bankrupt. The sermon contrasts earthly priorities (wealth, career, comfort) with heavenly treasures and calls believers to redirect their talents, time, and resources toward God's kingdom and eternity. Joshua Heward-Mills urges Christians to be "rich toward God" by investing in soul-winning and spiritual growth rather than pursuing temporary earthly comforts that cannot satisfy or endure.

514AOEJm0t8.title: Jesus and the man at the pool of Bethesda - Joshua Heward-Mills
514AOEJm0t8.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at a service in what appears to be an international location (references to Manila, Ghana, Jerusalem visits).
514AOEJm0t8.summary: Jesus encounters a man paralyzed for 38 years at the Pool of Bethesda and asks if he wants to be made whole, teaching that Jesus breaks cycles and processes when we cannot achieve breakthrough ourselves. The sermon identifies five types of infirmities represented by the pool's porches—impotence, blindness, lameness, withering, and unnamed problems—reflecting how Christians face various spiritual and practical powerlessness. The ultimate message is that while Jesus heals physical infirmities, the greater miracle is salvation from sin and its eternal consequences, with the closing exhortation that all breakthrough comes through Jesus, not human effort or ability.

WFeqnnqiTXw.title: Jesus at the Wedding - Joshua Heward-Mills
WFeqnnqiTXw.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching; auto-captions with minimal punctuation; scripture references spoken aloud
topics: Jesus at the Wedding in Cana
Faith and patience in following God
The first miracle and spiritual discernment
WFeqnnqiTXw.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches on Jesus's first miracle at the wedding in Cana (John 2), emphasizing that obedience to God's instruction—even when it seems irrelevant to the problem—is the master key to experiencing God's power and provision. He stresses that believers must recognize small miracles of sufficiency as the foundation for seeing greater miracles, and illustrates through personal testimony how following God's direction (though it makes no worldly sense) requires faith, patience, and the willingness to complete the entire process of obedience without seeing immediate results.

cYHVdlnY130.title: Jesus And The Man Sick of Palsy - Joshua Heward-Mills
cYHVdlnY130.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching; mentions his father Bishop Dag is not present today
topics: Four friends carrying the sick man
scriptures: Hebrews (church as New Jerusalem, chapter not specified)
cYHVdlnY130.summary: Jesus encounters a paralyzed man (sick of palsy) whose friends lower him through the roof, illustrating how spiritual paralysis—loss of control over one's body and decisions—affects those separated from God. Jesus identifies the man's core need not as physical healing but as spiritual restoration: to become God's son (addressing spiritual fatherlessness) and to have his sins forgiven, which is the deepest human problem that separates us from God. The message emphasizes that reaching Jesus requires overcoming obstacles like pride and the pressure of others, and that only a personal encounter with Christ—not family, friends, or circumstances—can truly transform and save a person.

d3he1eNep_I.title: Jesus and The House On the Rock - Joshua Heward-Mills
d3he1eNep_I.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at City Church
topics: Building on the rock vs sand
d3he1eNep_I.summary: Jesus teaches that merely confessing Him as Lord or doing outwardly impressive works does not guarantee entry into heaven; what matters is building your life on the rock—living according to God's word and will. Life brings inevitable storms from all directions—financial loss, broken relationships, death, illness—and only those whose foundation is Christ and Scripture will stand firm. Christians must allow God's wisdom to guide every area of life: marriage, work, finances, and character, rather than seeking security in money, relationships, or worldly success.

yJ0efi4leqs.title: Jesus and the Treasure in the Field - Joshua Heward-Mills
yJ0efi4leqs.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at City Church
yJ0efi4leqs.summary: Jesus taught that the Kingdom of Heaven is like a treasure hidden in a field, where a man sells all he has to buy the field—representing how Christ purchased the entire world through His sacrifice because humanity is His treasure. God is obsessed with people, seeing value in them despite their sin and brokenness, much like a dusty violin that becomes invaluable with the master's touch. Believers are encouraged to recognize their own worth in God's eyes and to value others as treasures that God is polishing to become His jewels.

pfIVKYO-QIM.title: I Am The Way  - Joshua Heward-Mills
pfIVKYO-QIM.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at his church
pfIVKYO-QIM.keyScripture: John 14:6 - I am the way, the truth, and the life: no man cometh unto the Father, but by me
pfIVKYO-QIM.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills expounds on Jesus's declaration "I am the way, the truth, and the life" from John 14:6, explaining that Jesus is not merely a path but a person who is the only way to God the Father. He contrasts the deceptions of Satan throughout history with the complete truth and abundant life found in Christ, illustrating how worldly pursuits like money, relationships, and education cannot satisfy the deep longing for heaven and fullness of joy that only relationship with Jesus provides. The sermon emphasizes that heaven is prepared as a home for believers, and salvation is a continuous work of God requiring surrender to Christ's lordship and a genuine encounter with Him.

BBOqToFkmP0.title: Jesus, the First and the Last - Joshua Heward-Mills
BBOqToFkmP0.preacherNote: Video title confirms Joshua Heward-Mills; transcript shows consistent first-person teaching throughout.
BBOqToFkmP0.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills preaches on Jesus Christ as 'the First and the Last,' emphasizing that Jesus is the only Savior, the only mediator between God and man, and holds the keys to death and hell. He illustrates this truth through personal testimonies and Scripture, calling listeners to abandoned life and repentance, stressing that missing this salvation is eternally fatal. The sermon culminates in altar calls for conversion and healing prayer, demonstrating Jesus' power over sickness and death.

s3uuEEhl4lA.title: Jesus and the Blind Man at the pool of Siloam - Joshua Heward-Mills
s3uuEEhl4lA.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at what appears to be a church service in Madina
topics: Jesus as the light of the world
Walking by faith not by sight
scriptures: Ephesians (referenced but specific verse not specified)
s3uuEEhl4lA.summary: Jesus encounters a man blind from birth and heals him by making clay, anointing his eyes, and commanding him to wash in the pool of Siloam—a story illustrating spiritual blindness to the gospel. The sermon emphasizes that many are spiritually blinded by the god of this world and cannot see the obvious love and sacrifice of Jesus despite hearing the gospel repeatedly. Joshua appeals to listeners to walk by faith, be reconciled to God, and transition from being 'almost persuaded' to fully surrendering their lives to Christ.

EoOkm38dsjE.title: THE VOICE OF THE HOLY SPIRIT - Joshua Heward-Mills
EoOkm38dsjE.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills (BJosh) preaching at his church
EoOkm38dsjE.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that hearing the voice of the Holy Spirit is the primary way to develop intimacy with God and follow His specific guidance for daily life. He explains that most Christians miss God's current plan because they rely only on sermons rather than cultivating their own personal relationship with the Holy Spirit, creating distance between God and His people. The sermon outlines multiple ways God communicates—through visions, inner conviction to the soul/mind, remembrance of Scripture, and through His servants—emphasizing that spiritual maturity develops through practice and exercise of spiritual senses to distinguish God's voice from other influences.

5ONSsXlxex0.title: Transformation - Joshua Heward-Mills
5ONSsXlxex0.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at a collaborative church event; he thanks Pastor Kofi for the invitation.
topics: The word of God as weapon
5ONSsXlxex0.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches on Christian transformation through three Greek words for change (metamorphoo, metaschematizo, and spiritual growth), emphasizing that true change requires renewing the mind through God's living word, not merely outward conformity. He diagnoses that many church members remain unchanged because they either neglect Scripture reading and meditation, lack the Holy Spirit's illumination to remove the veil over their hearts, or fail to work and act upon what they hear. The word of God is alive and powerful—sharper than any sword—capable of dividing soul from spirit and exposing the thoughts and intents of the heart, transforming believers from glory to glory.

yu8IlfRoxR4.title: The Strange Woman  - Joshua Heward-Mills
yu8IlfRoxR4.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching; his father Bishop Dag Heward-Mills mentioned as away in Manila, Philippines
yu8IlfRoxR4.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches on recognizing and avoiding 'strange women'—those who lead believers into sexual sin and spiritual danger. Using Proverbs 5-7, he identifies key signs including: operating in darkness, wearing immodest attire, maintaining a history of seduction, and belonging to ungodly peer groups. The solution lies in hiding God's Word in your heart, guarding what you see (eyes), touch (hands), and feel (heart) through Scripture, and maintaining awareness that such temptations are designed traps deeper and more destructive than they initially appear.

74-FfDYGsrU.title: How You Can Be In The Perfect Will Of God - City Church - Joshua Heward-Mills
74-FfDYGsrU.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at City Church
74-FfDYGsrU.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches on how Christians can discover and live in God's perfect will through hearing His voice and practicing obedience. He emphasizes that God has a specific plan for every believer's life—regarding marriage, career, and major decisions—and that prosperity comes only through seeking God's counsel first before making choices. The sermon warns that disobedience to God leads to brokenness and failure, while alignment with His will brings peace, promotion, and true welfare.

EFse4SQQ24s.title: 3 Poisons of the Tongue - Joshua Heward-Mills
EFse4SQQ24s.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching; Bishop Richard Dag Heward-Mills mentioned as absent but not preaching
EFse4SQQ24s.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches on three poisons of the tongue: expressing doubt (which kills faith and destiny), murmuring and complaining (which nullifies good works), and blasphemy against the Holy Spirit (speaking irreverently about God's work and servants). He emphasizes that words have life-and-death power, framing reality and determining outcomes, and calls believers to control their tongues by speaking faith, gratitude, and respect toward God and His church.

LHZQGJDljWw.title: Demons and how to Deal with them - Joshua Heward-Mills
topics: Leadership and the light of the church
Absence of light breeds demonic activity
LHZQGJDljWw.summary: Demons thrive in spiritual darkness (absence of God's word and light) and operate through specific influences like fear, depression, and jealousy that people often mistake for mere emotions rather than demonic spirits. The antidote to demonic activity is consistently exposing oneself to God's word through preaching, podcasts, prayer, and quiet time, which strengthens believers with power, love, and a sound mind to resist and overcome these spirits. Fear specifically is a demonic spirit that robs believers of power and prevents them from fulfilling God's calling, while faith and obedience to God's direction (even when afraid) demonstrates the opposite of the spirit of fear that Satan promotes.

M-pqT5fIhEg.title: Checks To Hearing From God - Joshua Heward-Mills
M-pqT5fIhEg.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches three essential checks for confirming God's guidance: seeking counsel from a multitude of wise counselors (not acting in isolation), proving all things by Scripture and discernment (especially regarding prophecies which can be mistaken), and waiting for confirmation through two or three witnesses. He emphasizes that even with clear spiritual signs like visions or dreams, believers must subject their decisions to biblical counsel and multiple confirmations to avoid going astray.

2jTJftgsN7Y.title: Demons and How To Deal With Them (WIS) - Joshua Heward-Mills
2jTJftgsN7Y.preacherNote: Video title confirms Joshua Heward-Mills; auto-captions transcribed with minimal punctuation
topics: The presence of God in corporate worship
The importance of community and team in faith
2jTJftgsN7Y.summary: Bishop Joshua addresses how demons hinder believers from living victoriously despite genuine conversion, using Romans 7:19 as the foundation that repeated sin indicates demonic presence rather than mere weakness. He emphasizes that the presence of God in corporate worship is where spiritual breakthrough occurs, and demons actively fight to isolate believers from church attendance and community. The solution is not self-improvement but recognizing demonic activity, submitting to God's presence corporately, and understanding that "where two or three gather in my name, I am there" (Matthew 18:20) as the battleground where spiritual victory is won.

vMcgOPlkzDk.title: Proton and Our Faults - Joshua Heward-Mills
vMcgOPlkzDk.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills (BJosh) speaking at a service with worship, prayer, and sermon teaching.
vMcgOPlkzDk.summary: Bishop Joshua emphasizes the gift of faith as the primary manifestation transforming Christian experience, urging believers to mix God's word with supernatural faith for true profit. He teaches extensively on Matthew 7:1-2, warning against judging others with harsh measurements and standards, using examples from Scripture of those judged unfairly (Moses with his Ethiopian wife, Stephen) to show God's defense of His servants. The central message urges spiritual maturity through restoration rather than condemnation, reminding believers that the same judgment they pass will be returned to them, and that only those who are spiritual should address others' faults with the aim of restoration.

RaLlzfpSwL8.title: How to Be a Spiritual Christian - Joshua Heward-Mills
RaLlzfpSwL8.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching; references his father Bishop Dag Heward-Mills' book 'How to Be a Strong Christian'
RaLlzfpSwL8.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches on becoming a spiritual Christian by allowing the Holy Spirit to dominate over fleshly desires through regular Christian fellowship and spiritual community. He emphasizes that believers are composed of body, soul, and spirit—and victory comes when the spirit man is strengthened through devotion to pastoral teaching, prayer, and the company of spiritually mature believers. The sermon warns against spiritual isolation and shows that separation from godly fellowship leads to spiritual defeat, using biblical examples and personal testimonies to demonstrate how Christian community is essential for spiritual strength.

UdP__aTKvXQ.title: Atmosphere  - Joshua Heward-Mills
UdP__aTKvXQ.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at what appears to be his home church
topics: What you hear, see, look upon, handle, and fellowship with
UdP__aTKvXQ.summary: Pastor Joshua teaches that your atmosphere—what you hear, see, look upon, handle, and fellowship with—determines who you become and shapes your spiritual development. Through Isaiah 61, he explains how proximity to God's presence, preaching, and righteous community transforms believers from broken-hearted captives into trees of righteousness and priests of God. He emphasizes that changing your fellowship, friends, media consumption, and environment is essential to removing spiritual idols and experiencing true freedom and transformation.

d0fE35y3sVw.title: Dont Lose Your Source of Power - Joshua Heward-Mills
d0fE35y3sVw.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at a youth service in Zurich
d0fE35y3sVw.summary: Joshua emphasizes that Christians lose their power through neglect of prayer and Scripture meditation. He teaches that prayer (especially in tongues) and daily engagement with God's Word are the primary sources of spiritual authority and effectiveness. Without these practices, believers become powerless against temptation, sickness, and worldly challenges—therefore Christians must actively pray, study Scripture, and shift their focus from earthly solutions to spiritual ones.

eOzg427hSkM.title: All About Fornication  - Joshua Heward-Mills
eOzg427hSkM.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching. Video includes worship, testimonies, and a sermon on fornication.
eOzg427hSkM.summary: Pastor Joshua teaches that fornication enslaves believers to sin, but knowledge of the truth—particularly understanding God's Word—sets people free. He emphasizes that Christians must stop being hypocritical about sexual sin, be honest about their struggles, and recognize that while fornication brings disgrace and wounds the heart, genuine repentance and knowledge of biblical truth enable complete freedom and transformation.

mu2s1nHSsQM.title: If you love the Lord you will obey him - Joshua Heward-Mills
mu2s1nHSsQM.preacherNote: Video title confirms Joshua Heward-Mills; auto-captions show characteristic preaching style and content structure.
mu2s1nHSsQM.keyScripture: Mark 12:30 - Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy mind, and with all thy strength
mu2s1nHSsQM.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that loving God with all your heart, soul, mind, and strength is the greatest and first commandment, surpassing all other Christian duties including prayer, missions, tithes, and abstinence. He emphasizes that love of God must be demonstrated through obedience and service, and that this love—not covetousness or self-seeking—is the master key that unlocks God's blessing and prosperity in a believer's life. The sermon illustrates how Solomon's prosperity flowed from his love for God, and how believers must reset their love and priorities to serve God's purpose rather than pursue worldly desires.

xuVepo-hEXA.title: If You Love the Lord, You’ll Love His Word - Joshua Heward-Mills
xuVepo-hEXA.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at his church
topics: Heart Condition Revealed Through Scripture Response
xuVepo-hEXA.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that loving God is demonstrated by loving His Word, using creative illustrations to show God's transcendence and how Scripture is the bridge between infinite God and finite humanity. He emphasizes that belief in Scripture's truth, submission to biblical authority in marriage and family, and honoring parents are essential markers of genuine love for the Lord. Through personal testimony and scriptural exposition, he calls believers to prioritize God's Word as their meditation and supreme guide for life decisions.

pir4c0SFTaQ.title: How to Make Your Faith Work - Joshua Heward-Mills
pir4c0SFTaQ.summary: Pastor Joshua teaches that faith is the substance of things hoped for, not merely a mental decision but a gift from God that grows through hearing His word. He explains that faith operates in measures—from little faith to great faith—and that genuine faith produces corresponding works, while faith without a clear conscience becomes shipwrecked. The core message emphasizes that believers substantiate their hopes (marriage, prosperity, family, travel) through faith developed by hearing God's word and making Spirit-directed decisions.

oQwRNrzMfac.title: The Secret of Directed Paths - Joshua Heward-Mills
oQwRNrzMfac.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills (BJosh) preaching at what appears to be a local congregation in Ghana
topics: The danger of leaning on own understanding
oQwRNrzMfac.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that God's plan for each believer's life is greater and more glorious than their current circumstances, and that following God's directed paths requires three foundational principles: trusting God completely, not leaning on one's own understanding, and acknowledging God in all decisions through prayer. He emphasizes that what seems right to human reasoning often leads to destruction, but surrendering to God's guidance—even when it contradicts personal logic or others' opinions—leads to increasing blessing and glory throughout life.

55raMOGR5L4.title: How To Become A Spiritual Christian 3 - Joshua Heward-Mills
topics: unity in the body of Christ
55raMOGR5L4.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that spiritual growth requires intentional fellowship with spiritually-minded believers, as company directly determines one's level of spirituality and character development. He emphasizes that even those with the Holy Spirit's presence must surround themselves with spiritual people, citing biblical examples like Mary needing Joseph's support and the 120 disciples achieving corporate anointing through unity. The sermon warns against maintaining friendships with carnal or worldly influences, explaining that wrong associations trap the soul through opinion formation and character corruption.

tlZVYy0mn60.title: Those Who Do Not Please God | Joshua Heward-Mills
tlZVYy0mn60.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching on pleasing God through examining what displeases Him
tlZVYy0mn60.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that despite spiritual privileges—God's presence, miracles, teachings, and church community—what truly displeases God is the lust and desires of the human heart toward worldly things. He emphasizes that covetousness is idolatry, and that the desires we harbor (for money, relationships, status, earthly pleasures) are the root causes that separate believers from God's pleasure, using Israel's wilderness rebellion as a cautionary example. The sermon calls believers to examine and mortify their sinful desires, trusting only in God rather than testing His ability to provide.

6i_dEnj40pk.title: God Sees Not As Man Sees | Joshua Heward-Mills
6i_dEnj40pk.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching; auto-captions with minimal punctuation
topics: Testing of faith versus testing of motives
Abraham's sacrifice as model of heart loyalty
Love of God as primary motivation
6i_dEnj40pk.keyScripture: 1 Samuel 16:7 - "The Lord seeth not as man seeth: for man looketh on the outward appearance, but the Lord looketh on the heart."
6i_dEnj40pk.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that God's assessment of believers differs fundamentally from human judgment—God sees hidden motives and secret actions that no one else observes, while humans judge only by outward appearance. Using Abraham's sacrifice of Isaac as the primary example, he demonstrates that God tests not faith (when promises are attached) but motive and love when no reward is promised, revealing whether believers love God Himself or merely His blessings. The sermon challenges believers to align their heart condition, private conduct, and inner motives with God's evaluation standards rather than seeking approval from others or self-assessment.

YfSkdCIE2U4.title: Alternatives To Pleasing God | Meeting God | Joshua Heward-Mills
YfSkdCIE2U4.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at 'Meeting God' Bible study session
YfSkdCIE2U4.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that the primary goal of every believer must be to please God, not themselves or other people. He identifies four main alternatives to pleasing God: living for yourself, living to please men, and emphasizes that human approval or disapproval should not determine one's spiritual destiny or obedience. The central message is that promotion, provision, and life outcomes come from God alone, not from any human authority, and believers must choose between the applause of men and the approval of God.

1q8pw9_QO5o.title: The Faith of the God Pleaser | Meeting God | Joshua Heward-Mills
1q8pw9_QO5o.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching on faith and pleasing God
1q8pw9_QO5o.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that true faith requires believing God exists with omniscience (knowing all things), omnipresence (being everywhere), and omnipotence (having all power), not merely confessing belief with one's mouth. He emphasizes that pleasing God is impossible without heart-level belief that God knows every thought, motive, and action in darkness, which should compel believers to live righteously regardless of human approval. The sermon stresses that true believers abandon self-reliance, stop resisting evil, and trust God's provision rather than using human schemes or stepping outside His will.

8EPWoAbcN1M.title: Pleasing God | Meeting God | Joshua Heward-Mills
8EPWoAbcN1M.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills (BJosh) - identified by title and self-reference ('me, heaven or earth, dead or alive... I live for one thing, to be pleasing')
topics: The axe laid to the root
Seeking God's approval over man's approval
8EPWoAbcN1M.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills introduces a new sermon series on 'Pleasing God,' emphasizing that a Christian's fundamental goal should shift from worldly ambitions (wealth, happiness, status, marriage) to the singular aim of pleasing God. He uses the principle of 'laying the axe to the root'—addressing the spiritual root causes of problems rather than symptoms—and contrasts the believer's new purpose in Christ with the goals of unbelievers. The central message is that one's life goals determine one's spiritual freedom, vulnerability to temptation, and ultimate fulfillment; only those whose constant ambition is to please God will experience true joy and avoid the sadness that comes from pursuing empty personal ambitions.

XdIq-EJ05-c.title: 8  Become a Shepherd Because People Must Be Visited and Stregnthened- Joshua Heward-Mills
XdIq-EJ05-c.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills delivering sermon; references to his father Bishop Dag Heward-Mills included for context of following/copying ministry models
XdIq-EJ05-c.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that every Christian must become a shepherd because people need visitation and spiritual strengthening. He emphasizes the importance of following and copying from spiritual mentors—what you follow, you become—and illustrates this through biblical examples like Jacob copying Esau and Peter healing the lame beggar. The core message is that believers must study what they have learned and received from leaders, implement it faithfully, and understand that the presence of God comes through obedience to studied truth, not personal innovation.

_67jZKeT4OM.title: 6  Become a Shepherd Because People are Spiritually Hungry - Joshua Heward-Mills
_67jZKeT4OM.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills (BJosh) preaching at First Love Centre
_67jZKeT4OM.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills emphasizes that people are spiritually hungry and need true shepherds to guide them away from deception, worldly lies, and false prophets who exploit believers. The role of a shepherd is to provide spiritual nourishment through truth, anointing, prayer, and the presence of God—not materialistic promises or manipulation. He calls believers to hunger for God's presence and genuine spiritual transformation rather than seeking solutions from false sources, illustrating this through worship and the experiential presence of the Holy Spirit.

dT1FZ1AyNPk.title: 2  Why You Can Become a Shepherd - Joshua Heward-Mills
dT1FZ1AyNPk.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at a camp leaders meeting
dT1FZ1AyNPk.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches leaders that the fivefold ministry gifts (apostles, prophets, evangelists, pastors, teachers) exist to perfect the saints for the work of ministry and to edify the body of Christ. He emphasizes that true shepherding is measured by the number of people one spiritually cares for and leads, not by administrative tasks or titles, and that unperfected saints cannot do the work of ministry effectively. The core message is that leaders must themselves be perfected through doctrine, reproof, correction, and instruction in order to perfect others and grow the church.

kvBJGD6mlUI.title: 5  Reasons Why You Can Become a Shepherd - Joshua Heward-Mills
kvBJGD6mlUI.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at a youth/training camp event with interactive audience engagement
topics: Balance between physical and spiritual growth
kvBJGD6mlUI.summary: Bishop Heward-Mills teaches on becoming a shepherd as a means to combine material and spiritual progress, warning against pursuing only earthly advancement while neglecting spiritual growth. He emphasizes that godliness with contentment is true gain, and that believers must advance simultaneously in both the physical realm and the spirit through shepherding roles. The sermon contrasts temporary earthly qualifications with eternal spiritual qualifications obtained through faithful ministry and discipleship.

mcTobRTaeRY.title: 1  What is a Shepherd - Joshua Heward-Mills
mcTobRTaeRY.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills (BJosh) preaching at a leadership camp on the gift of shepherding.
topics: Difference between teaching, preaching, healing and pastoring
mcTobRTaeRY.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that the gift of shepherding is distinct from and more critical than the gifts of teaching, preaching, or healing, using Matthew 9:35-36 to show Jesus observed scattered multitudes despite his powerful ministry. He emphasizes that pastors and leaders must develop the shepherding gift through care, visitation, and accountability to prevent their congregations from scattering, illustrated by the absence of church members at the leadership camp. The sermon challenges church leaders to recognize that they are failing their people not through lack of ministry skills but through the absence of genuine shepherd hearts and pastoral oversight.

nm-8XY-ig7U.title: 7  Offering and Ministration - Joshua Heward-Mills
nm-8XY-ig7U.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills leads worshippers in declaring the power and healing authority of Jesus' name, using the example of Paul and Silas in prison whose worship caused chains to break and the prison to shake. He emphasizes freedom from strongholds, demonic oppression, and negative thoughts through faith in Jesus' name and God's presence. The sermon includes appeals for offerings and concludes with ministry activation where participants experience the fire of God and are commissioned as anointed vessels.

o84m5Y6we7w.title: 4  Reasons Why You Can Become a Shepherd - Joshua Heward-Mills
o84m5Y6we7w.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching on shepherding and pastoral care
o84m5Y6we7w.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills presents four reasons Christians should become shepherds: there is a great need for shepherds in the church, people need practical mentoring beyond preaching alone, when there is no shepherd the sheep scatter and become vulnerable, and shepherding is essential to fulfilling the Great Commission through discipleship. He emphasizes that every Christian can be a shepherd by caring for small groups of people, providing personal guidance on life decisions, and helping believers grow spiritually through one-on-one relationships rather than relying solely on sermons and church programs.

sVwZidbsPO8.title: 9  Conditions to Becoming a Shepherd- Joshua Heward-Mills
sVwZidbsPO8.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching on conditions for becoming a shepherd in the church.
topics: Compassion for the ignorant and wayward
Restlessness and ambition in God's work
The calling of Aaron as example
sVwZidbsPO8.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills outlines nine conditions for becoming a shepherd, beginning with desire as the foundational condition that drives spiritual ambition and service. He emphasizes that a divine calling, comparable to Aaron's calling by God, is essential—no person can assume this honor themselves; God must ordain them. The shepherd must develop compassion for the ignorant and those who stray, viewing pastoral work not as punishment or hardship but as a profound honor and privilege that should inspire humility, dedication, and a restless spirit to grow God's kingdom.

uaa4-pYYVXc.title: 3  The Divine Call - Joshua Heward-Mills
topics: Ministry as God's call not choice
uaa4-pYYVXc.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills explains that every Christian is called by God to be a saint and equipped for ministry work, using the example of Samuel's calling to illustrate how God's voice may sound like a familiar human voice but originates from God. He emphasizes that pastoral responsibility is a divine calling, not a human choice, and that shepherds will answer to God for the souls entrusted to their care. The sermon challenges believers to recognize their calling and embrace their role in God's kingdom, regardless of personal limitations or preferences.

0t06iDZCS-s.title: 19  The Ministry, Work or Rest - Joshua Heward-Mills
0t06iDZCS-s.preacherNote: Video title confirms Joshua Heward-Mills; sermon content and teaching style consistent with his ministry
0t06iDZCS-s.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills contrasts the cultural negative associations with the word 'work' (homework, classwork, coursework) with the biblical concept of ministry as intentional, purposeful labour in God's kingdom. He emphasizes that shepherds must install a 'mind to work'—a conscious awareness of their employment in prayer, visitation, and counseling—rather than viewing church service as mere rest or entertainment. The central call is for believers to transition from being passive harvesters to active labourers, understanding that building God's church requires the committed, professional mindset of workers who know their job description and execute their duties with dedication and awareness.

7hW0Yx8G4Yk.title: 20  Characteristics of Work - Joshua Heward-Mills
7hW0Yx8G4Yk.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at a camp setting with interactive audience participation and personal testimonies
7hW0Yx8G4Yk.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches the twenty characteristics of work in ministry, emphasizing that work has defined hours, requires sustained energy and sacrifice, expands beyond scheduled times, and demands the mind of a self-motivated laborer. He contrasts the initial "fun stage" of ministry work with mature commitment, using workplace examples and personal testimonies to show that true work is repetitive, regular, and done without constant supervision. The core message is that spiritual work—like physical work—demands respect for time, discipline, and understanding that souls perishing necessitates consistent, sacrificial labor modeled after God the Father's own work.

9XYmiigwReE.title: 21  Offering and Worship - Joshua Heward-Mills
9XYmiigwReE.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills leading offering and worship session
9XYmiigwReE.summary: Bishop Joshua leads a worship and offering session, calling believers to give special offerings and surrender their lives to God. He emphasizes the theme of offering oneself completely to the Lord through worship, prayer, and financial giving. The session concludes with prayers of blessing, declaring that God is promoting, anointing, and lifting up His people.

CQvV1W2a5rA.title: 12  Conditions to Becoming a Shepherd pt.4
CQvV1W2a5rA.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaching part 4 of '12 Conditions to Becoming a Shepherd' series at a camp meeting.
CQvV1W2a5rA.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that a shepherd's wife must be honorable, grave, not slanderous, and most critically—sober-minded, meaning uninfluenced by worldly fashions, feminism, tribal loyalties, or cultural trends. The wife must be guided solely by the Holy Spirit and God's Word, not swayed by external movements or secular ideologies. The pastor illustrates through contrast how a wife influenced by Western materialism, tribal politics, feminist movements, or cultural divisions becomes a hindrance rather than a help to her husband's shepherding calling.

Jq_M1Jz6jKI.title: 15  Take Up the Spiritual Burden - Joshua Heward-Mills
Jq_M1Jz6jKI.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at a camp session
Jq_M1Jz6jKI.summary: Bishop Heward-Mills teaches that spiritual power and effective ministry flow from a genuine spiritual burden—compassion and concern for people—rather than from mere knowledge or mechanics. He illustrates through Jesus' healings and feeding of the multitude that when a pastor is moved with compassion and burden for their congregation, the power of God manifests and people respond. The primary requirement for effective shepherding is developing a heart burden for the people God has called you to serve, which compels intercessory prayer, genuine care, and sacrificial leadership.

KNlN6TEZJsE.title: 17  15 Essential Features of a Shepherd - Joshua Heward-Mills
KNlN6TEZJsE.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaching on shepherding qualities.
KNlN6TEZJsE.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills outlines 15 essential features of a shepherd, emphasizing preaching excellence, evangelistic fervor, loyalty, and proper conduct in opposite-sex relationships. A critical point stressed is treating the opposite sex as family (brothers and sisters) to avoid moral compromise and maintaining integrity. The sermon underscores that unfaithfulness and disloyalty disqualify one from shepherding, citing Jesus's recognition that Judas was an unfaithful disciple among the twelve.

KYApS8CnlOI.title: 16  Poimen - Joshua Heward-Mills
KYApS8CnlOI.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills explains the Greek word 'Poimen' (Shepherd), which Jesus used to describe Himself and which forms the basis of pastoral ministry. He emphasizes that just as professionals specialize in their skills, pastors must specialize in shepherding and pastoral care rather than trying to do everything—distinguishing pastoral ministry from prophetic, evangelistic, and apostolic callings. The core responsibility of a good shepherd is to lead, restore souls, comfort the broken-hearted, and guide people toward spiritual maturity and purpose.

XkwoUyIbYbA.title: 14  Why Leading People is a Burden- Joshua Heward-Mills
XkwoUyIbYbA.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaching on leadership burdens
XkwoUyIbYbA.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills identifies seven reasons why leading people is a burden: ungrateful and forgetful followers, betrayal by close associates, wickedness and theft, sudden abandonment, disrespect and impudence, disobedience, and those who don't understand or respond. He emphasizes that leaders cannot carry these burdens alone and must develop other leaders by pouring into and training empty vessels. The ultimate goal is to become a chief shepherd who multiplies spiritual leaders rather than trying to be a superman doing everything alone.

aXrVEfKaEb0.title: 18  15 Essential Features of a Shepherd pt.2 - Joshua Heward-Mills
aXrVEfKaEb0.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching part 2 of 15 Essential Features of a Shepherd
topics: Catching the spirit of the leader
aXrVEfKaEb0.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches essential shepherd qualities including developing preaching skills through copying exemplary ministers, cultivating a burning heart for the lost, and maintaining proper relationships with others (especially opposite sex) by treating them as family members. He emphasizes the critical importance of loyalty, faithfulness, financial stability, and personal holiness as non-negotiable requirements for effective pastoral ministry.

c24-CBCGH3I.title: 13  Sharing The Burden- Joshua Heward-Mills
c24-CBCGH3I.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at a leadership camp on burden-sharing and delegation among church leaders.
c24-CBCGH3I.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches how pastoral burden is shared when leaders receive God's spirit and stand together with their prophet. Using Moses' struggle with the weight of leading 600,000 people, he shows that seventy elders empowered by God's spirit can shoulder the burden together. The solution to burnout in ministry is not more titles or structure, but a genuine impartation of the anointing that moves leaders to actively serve alongside their shepherd.

lURiaUtOI0k.title: 22  How to be a Good Shepherd - Joshua Heward-Mills
lURiaUtOI0k.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches twelve essential characteristics of a good shepherd, emphasizing that leaders must know their sheep by name, lead by example, keep their voice known to their flock, and sacrificially give their whole lives for their people. Drawing from John 10 and other scriptures, he contrasts good shepherds with hired hands, stressing intimate knowledge of congregation members, protection from spiritual threats, and maintaining church unity. The sermon concludes with an altar call for spiritual anointing, encouraging pastors to seek advancement to the next level of spiritual gifting and ministerial effectiveness.

psgvtOLwPPI.title: 10  Conditions to Becoming a Shepherd pt.2- Joshua Heward-Mills
psgvtOLwPPI.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that a shepherd must demonstrate willing commitment beyond mere desire or acknowledgment of calling. Using the analogy of his unfulfilled passion for violin versus his actual investment in guitar and ministry, he contrasts empty words and visions with genuine commitment shown through financial sacrifice, time investment, and disciplined action. True shepherding requires proving commitment through concrete investments in books, prayer, meetings, training camps, and financial resources—not merely talking about wanting to serve.

vKjJVuqCly4.title: 11  Conditions to Becoming a Shepherd pt.3- Joshua Heward-Mills
vKjJVuqCly4.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching on pastoral qualifications and character development.
topics: Testing of character through time, fire, and distance
Friends of Paul (types of church members)
vKjJVuqCly4.summary: A shepherd's qualification depends on godly character tested through three primary trials: time, fire (crisis), and distance (relocation). True pastoral fitness is revealed not by initial gifting or charm, but by how one responds to correction, maintains humility under pressure, and demonstrates faithfulness in small responsibilities over extended periods. The sermon uses Paul's relationships with various ministry partners (Demas, Luke, Mark, Priscilla and Aquila) to illustrate the different types of church members and workers, emphasizing that steadfast faithfulness is ultimately more valuable than natural ability.

lCTn_Vtk3HA.title: 1  Why You Should Avoid a Pseudo Ministry - Joshua Heward-Mills
lCTn_Vtk3HA.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at a pastoral ministry camp
lCTn_Vtk3HA.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills contrasts genuine pastoral ministry (gold) with pseudo ministry (brass), teaching that the five-fold gifts—apostles, prophets, evangelists, pastors, and teachers—are the true offices God established for the church. He emphasizes that a pastor's core work is prayer and ministry of the word, warning against distractions like church politics, preference-based divisions, and administrative tasks that pull leaders away from their primary calling. The message calls pastors to transformation, purity, and consistency in ministry, reminiscent of how God required pure gold in the tabernacle.

ONxom_tQZGY.title: 2  How You Can Achieve Excellence in Ministry - Joshua Heward-Mills
ONxom_tQZGY.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills (BJosh) - Video title confirms authorship
ONxom_tQZGY.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches how to achieve excellence in ministry by emulating Jesus Christ's example through specific spiritual marks and traits. He emphasizes that true pastoral excellence is demonstrated through consistent prayer life (morning prayers, strong crying, all-night intercession), personal visitation of church members in their homes, and intentional interaction—not through external metrics like stadium size or social media presence. The sermon challenges pastors at all levels to press toward the high calling of Christ-like ministry through discipline, intercession, and genuine shepherding care.

UyH25mODOyg.title: 3  Ten Reasons Why Every Minister Must Pray - Joshua Heward-Mills
UyH25mODOyg.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching on prayer and pastoral ministry
topics: Prayer as an act of faith
Faith and belief in God's attributes
UyH25mODOyg.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches on prayer as the foundational act of faith for ministers, emphasizing that prayer demonstrates belief in God's omnipresence, omniscience, and omnipotence. He contrasts trust in prayer with misplaced faith in meetings, strategies, and human effort, arguing that pastoral authority and effectiveness derive from intimacy with God through prayer. The sermon stresses that visible spiritual authority and results come from leaders who prioritize seeking God rather than relying on organizational tactics.

wAFnILSlMio.title: 5  Twelve Reasons Why Pastors Must Visit Their Sheep - Joshua Heward-Mills
wAFnILSlMio.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching to what appears to be a pastoral training class
wAFnILSlMio.summary: Pastors must prioritize visitation as a core shepherding function, not optional ministry. Every church member deserves and needs pastoral visits to their homes, which are essential for spiritual health, member retention, and preventing abandonment to Satan. Visitation is a learnable skill developed through consistent practice that overcomes initial social awkwardness and demonstrates genuine pastoral care.
