3q651LWnI4M.title: 6  Eight Guidelines for Fruitful Visitation - Joshua Heward-Mills
3q651LWnI4M.preacherNote: Video title confirms 'Joshua Heward-Mills'; teaching style and content consistent with BJosh
3q651LWnI4M.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches eight essential guidelines for effective pastoral visitation, emphasizing that every visit must have a clear spiritual purpose and accomplish something specific. He uses Jesus Christ as the primary model, showing how Christ balanced social connection with spiritual impact through prayer, the ministry of God's word, and anointing. The central message is that skilled visitors must combine relaxed social engagement with powerful spiritual authority, ensuring that visits transform lives and have lasting eternal impact.

BX84F-_8AiU.title: 7  Ten Rules for Visitation - Joshua Heward-Mills
BX84F-_8AiU.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills (BJosh) delivering sermon on pastoral visitation principles.
BX84F-_8AiU.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills presents ten rules for pastoral visitation, emphasizing that pastors must never refuse visit requests and must discern unspoken needs through spiritual sensitivity and observation. He instructs that visitations should include both routine scheduled visits and urgent responses to special circumstances, and should typically be conducted in pairs for safety and accountability. The sermon uses biblical examples and personal anecdotes to illustrate how effective shepherding requires knowing members' real circumstances and intervening before spiritual crises drive them away from the church.

fWKeKjcmIxM.title: 8  Three Secrets Behind the Power of Visitation - Joshua Heward-Mills
fWKeKjcmIxM.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills (BJosh) preaching at a camp session on visitation ministry.
fWKeKjcmIxM.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that powerful visitation operates through three supernatural keys: ministering the living word of God, showing genuine love, and praying with faith and authority. The word of God is alive and active, piercing the soul and spirit to separate fleshly desires from spiritual truth, and serves as the only true spiritual weapon against demonic opposition. When visiting homes, believers must operate in the anointing of the Holy Spirit, speaking scriptures with power and binding spirits of depression, sickness, lust, and death through the authority given in Christ.

tx4kY9KUdsA.title: 9  The Visits of Jesus Christ - Joshua Heward-Mills
tx4kY9KUdsA.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching on the visits of Jesus Christ
tx4kY9KUdsA.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches on the five primary visits of Jesus Christ: salvation, healing, miracles, teaching/advice, and rebukes, demonstrating how Jesus engaged in personal home visitations. He emphasizes that faith is foundational to healing and miracles, and that believers must be anointed by the Holy Spirit to carry out effective visitation ministry. The sermon concludes with an impartation of anointing for pastoral and visitation ministry, encouraging pastors to model Christ's personal, transformative approach to ministry.

SmXu3e855O8.title: 10  Eight Reasons Why Interaction is Important for Every Church - Joshua Heward-Mills
SmXu3e855O8.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at a church camp on interaction and relationships
SmXu3e855O8.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches eight reasons why interaction is essential for church growth and member retention: it makes people stay, prevents nets from breaking, makes people feel important and valued as more than statistics, counteracts wrong impressions through personal contact, and increases commitment. Jesus exemplified this relational approach with His disciples through personal conversation, fellowship, and genuine care, demonstrating that spiritual transformation requires both the Word and genuine community connection.

swBZYnynvqw.title: 11  How to Interact With People - Joshua Heward-Mills
swBZYnynvqw.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills (BJosh) - confirmed by video title and content
swBZYnynvqw.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches pastors and church leaders how to effectively interact with people through the metaphor of deep sea fishing. Drawing primarily from Luke 5, he emphasizes that pastors must make conscious decisions to engage even with strangers, maintain spiritual purity (clean nets), launch out with boldness into uncomfortable situations, work in partnership, and ultimately bring people from spiritual isolation into the church community where Jesus is present. The core lesson is that meaningful pastoral interaction requires intentional effort, reliance on the Holy Spirit, teamwork, and the conviction that church attendance itself is spiritually transformative for believers.

VXFxwzySIaw.title: 14  How to Achieve Excellence in Ministry - Joshua Heward-Mills
VXFxwzySIaw.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaching at a pastoral ministry campus
topics: Dangers of spiritual fullness and complacency
Following Jesus as the model pastor
The four aspects of Jesus' ministry
Building up the body of Christ
VXFxwzySIaw.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that pastors must approach ministry with humility and hunger rather than complacency, warning against spiritual fullness that prevents growth. He uses Jesus as the model pastor—demonstrating prayer, visitation, counseling, and teaching—and emphasizes that the goal is to become complete like Jesus through receiving grace upon grace. The core of transforming one's pastoral ministry is understanding and implementing the four aspects of Jesus' ministry while continuously praying for hunger and emptiness to receive more of God's fullness.

k-1lrMVM86A.title: 15  Eighteen Facts About Spiritual Warfare - Joshua Heward-Mills
k-1lrMVM86A.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at a camp session on spiritual warfare.
k-1lrMVM86A.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches eighteen facts about spiritual warfare, establishing that Satan—not human opposition—is the principal enemy of the church. He emphasizes the necessity of knowing Satan's nature, tactics, and deception to effectively combat him through prayer, intercession, and wrestling spiritually for church members. Pastors must recognize demonic activity in their congregations and take aggressive spiritual action through intercession rather than relying on fleshly or administrative strategies.

TAopyZ-NcsQ.title: 16  Two Steps to Understanding Intercession - Joshua Heward-Mills
TAopyZ-NcsQ.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches two steps to understanding intercession: (1) to modify the course of events through prayer before circumstances occur, and (2) to stand in the gap between God and people as a mediator. He emphasizes that pastors must intercede specifically for their assigned members by name, following Christ's example in John 17, rather than praying generally for the world. This ministry of intercession is distinct from preaching or administration and involves standing in the spiritual gap to prevent Satan's plans from succeeding in members' lives.

I0O5hCudZtY.title: 17  How to Intercede Against the 12 Laws - Joshua Heward-Mills
I0O5hCudZtY.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches on intercession and the twelve spiritual laws that work against Christians, using scripture to equip pastors and church leaders to stand in the gap for their members. He emphasizes that intercessory prayer can turn away God's wrath, prevent spiritual deterioration, and transform the course of events in believers' lives. The sermon calls Christians to embrace the ministry of intercession as their highest calling, modeled after Jesus who ever lives to make intercession for the saints.

1tNsiH6A7zQ.title: 18  Seven Examples of Praying Fervently - Joshua Heward-Mills
1tNsiH6A7zQ.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills presents seven biblical examples of fervent prayer to motivate pastors and believers toward intercessory ministry. Drawing from Elijah, Hannah, Jacob, Moses, David, the early church, and Jesus, he demonstrates how persistent, passionate prayer produces spiritual transformation and breakthrough. The sermon emphasizes that churches experience growth and the Holy Spirit's power through the dedication of intercessors who refuse to stop praying until God's will is established.

N4S-rk6eCs0.title: 19 Seven Reasons Why Listening to Tapes or Messages will Transform Your Ministry-Joshua Heward-Mills
N4S-rk6eCs0.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at a ministry camp on listening to teaching messages
N4S-rk6eCs0.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches seven reasons why listening to preaching messages and tapes will transform ministry, emphasizing that the anointing is transferred through hearing anointed teaching. He uses personal testimonies and scripture to demonstrate how listening has enabled him to know scriptures, hear God's voice, and experience God's presence in fellowship. The core message is that consistent, devoted listening to anointed messages from spiritual leaders imparts grace and anointing that enables preachers to replicate the same transformative power in their own ministries.

6_zvNOj62RU.title: 20  15 Keys to Becoming a Successful Teacher of the Word- Joshua Heward-Mills
6_zvNOj62RU.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching on keys to becoming a successful teacher of the Word
6_zvNOj62RU.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches fifteen keys to becoming a successful teacher of God's Word, emphasizing constant spiritual preparation, meditation on Scripture, and personal transformation. He stresses that effective teaching requires being transformed inwardly (metamorphosis), maintaining daily spiritual disciplines, and studying to show oneself approved to God. The sermon focuses on how preachers must be ready in and out of season, use illustrations and stories like Jesus did, and measure success by life-changing impact on their audience.

z2NaxjnkJrE.title: 22  Seven Reason Why a Pastor Should Teach in Series - Joshua Heward-Mills
z2NaxjnkJrE.preacherNote: Video title confirms Joshua Heward-Mills as speaker
z2NaxjnkJrE.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills explains seven reasons pastors should teach in sermon series rather than isolated messages. Using the feeding of the five thousand as a central metaphor, he demonstrates how breaking one long message into smaller weekly portions sustains congregation attendance, maintains freshness in delivery, creates anticipation, and allows thorough coverage of important biblical topics. Series teaching builds dependency on the pastor's regular feeding of the flock and prevents spiritual complacency.

gBgoMu8yBcQ.title: 23  Seven Reasons Why You Should Teach Your Leaders - Joshua Heward-Mills
gBgoMu8yBcQ.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches seven reasons why pastors must invest in teaching their leaders rather than only focusing on general congregation. Teaching leaders multiplies ministry impact, establishes pastoral authority, creates investment in the church's future, and follows the example of Jesus Christ who invested heavily in discipling the twelve. Leaders who are properly trained and equipped will expand the ministry explosively, reproduce disciples, and take the gospel to all nations.

fUO9dos8nKw.title: 24  Four Keys to Augment Your Teaching Ministry - Joshua Heward-Mills
fUO9dos8nKw.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills teaching at a conference/camp session
fUO9dos8nKw.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches four keys to augment teaching ministry: understanding full-time ministry dedication, practicing teaching without notes by memorizing three cardinal points with supporting scriptures, building a treasure box of teachings from various sources, and teaching regularly to develop spiritual discernment. He emphasizes that God's word brings wisdom, happiness, and spiritual maturity, and demonstrates this through practical examples of sermon construction. The development of teaching skills requires consistent practice and exposure to diverse spiritual content beyond one's prepared messages.

m6tyqMUw3H8.title: 25  Nine Reasons Why Pastors Should Preach the Word of God - Joshua Heward-Mills
m6tyqMUw3H8.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at a camp session
m6tyqMUw3H8.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills presents nine reasons pastors must preach God's Word: it provides direction, brings light in darkness, imparts wisdom, teaches what to do, enlightens understanding of life circumstances, purges sin, convicts stubborn hearts, saves souls through the gospel, and penetrates and transforms the innermost thoughts of congregants. He emphasizes that preaching the Word itself—not miracles or entertainment—is the true power of God and a pastor's primary responsibility. Throughout, he illustrates these points with personal stories and scriptural foundations, calling pastors to faithful, consistent proclamation of Scripture.

GcamB9A2hQQ.title: 120 Reasons Why You Must be a Soul Winner - Joshua Heward-Mills
GcamB9A2hQQ.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills speaking directly to his congregation about personal evangelism and soul-winning.
GcamB9A2hQQ.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that soul-winning is the central mandate from Jesus Christ (the Great Commission), emphasizing that all Christians are called to it though few are chosen to actively engage. He argues that personal evangelism—though appearing weak and foolish to the world—is the most glorious work and essential to both personal faith and church growth. He calls his congregation to return to direct, person-to-person gospel sharing as the primary work of ministry, illustrated through personal testimony and the example of Billy Graham's simplicity and effectiveness.

JL0Tn8IaX1Q.title: Your Faith Your Life - Joshua Heward-Mills
JL0Tn8IaX1Q.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at what appears to be a Bible school or ministry training event.
topics: The anointing of the Holy Spirit
JL0Tn8IaX1Q.summary: Bishop Joshua emphasizes that faith is demonstrated through actions and lifestyle, not mere words or confessions. He argues that your current level of faith is revealed by how you live—your choices in prayer, giving, service, and obedience—and that this faith level directly determines your spiritual fruitfulness and ability to accomplish God's work. The sermon challenges believers to align their actions with their professed beliefs, using his own life as testimony that genuine faith produces tangible results in ministry, finances, and spiritual power.

NzaRqof29VU.title: Not By Might Nor By Power But By My Spirit - Joshua Heward-Mills
NzaRqof29VU.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills speaking at his church (appears to be Lighthouse Chapel International based on context)
topics: Not by might nor by power but by Spirit
Accessing the anointing through prayer and intimacy with God
The role of the prophet and transference of spirits
Stepping out in faith into the supernatural
Blaming results on spiritual anointing not circumstances
NzaRqof29VU.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaches that spiritual growth and church expansion are accomplished not through human effort, might, or power but solely through God's Spirit and the anointing. He challenges leaders to stop making excuses based on circumstances (distance, venue, members) and instead recognize that spiritual results require seeking the anointing through prayer, intimacy with God, and operating in the supernatural realm rather than relying on natural methods. He encourages believers to step out of the boat of natural reasoning into supernatural ministry, illustrating that true transformation happens when one accesses spiritual keys from anointed leaders and allows God to work supernaturally in their life and ministry.

O1YVgm6kR_o.title: Keys to the Harvest Experience - Joshua Heward-Mills
O1YVgm6kR_o.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills delivers the main sermon; another pastor (introduced as 'Joseph' from Kumasi) provides a secondary exhortation on evangelism.
O1YVgm6kR_o.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that the spiritual harvest is already ripe and ready for reaping, requiring believers to shift from sowing mentality to reaping readiness. The foundation for effective harvest gathering is massive organized prayer, fasting, and outreach—mobilizing entire congregations, not just clergy, in coordinated intercession and evangelism. He emphasizes that every believer in Christ has received the ministry of reconciliation and must actively perceive and pursue souls, as prayer combined with systematic evangelistic action is the key to church growth and spiritual fruit-bearing.

oo4Vbzm0f7o.title: Jesus Christ Son Of Man - Joshua Heward-Mills
oo4Vbzm0f7o.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at one of his father Bishop Dag Heward-Mills' churches
topics: Following Jesus as the greatest pastor
oo4Vbzm0f7o.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that Jesus Christ, calling Himself the Son of Man, is the greatest pastor and example for all ministers. He emphasizes that to enter ministry, one must accept humility and follow Jesus' pattern of submission, scriptural grounding, and passing spiritual tests through flesh control and obedience. The sermon challenges pastors to learn from Jesus' example rather than seeking shortcuts, to study Scripture deeply, and to submit to authority as Jesus did, warning that pride and pride prevent anointing and spiritual promotion.

yzjeIhSWXHw.title: How to Become a Good Shepherd - Joshua Heward-Mills
yzjeIhSWXHw.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at a Bible School
yzjeIhSWXHw.summary: A good shepherd must first follow Jesus closely as the ultimate Good Shepherd and develop a personal, living relationship with Him. Second, a good shepherd must influence and change people's minds through the Word of God and the power of the Holy Spirit, not merely through external rules or instructions. Third, a good shepherd must continuously preach, teach, and heal, providing fresh spiritual food and revelation to the flock rather than recycling old material.

cUuOUKw46NI.title: Revelation - Joshua Heward-Mills
cUuOUKw46NI.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching on church growth, spiritual maturity, and the importance of revelation in ministry.
topics: Word of God Growth vs Multiplication
cUuOUKw46NI.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that church growth depends on leaders taking heed to themselves through prayer, intercession, and revelation rather than focusing on external circumstances. He emphasizes that spiritual growth (oxano) and numerical multiplication (plethuno) come from leaders first receiving fresh revelation from God through the Holy Spirit, who teaches, guides into truth, and shows things to come. The congregation experiences transformation when pastors are genuinely engaged with God's presence and operate from current divine guidance, not just programs or techniques.

RQjB6zSMXZ8.title: Anointing - Joshua Heward-Mills
RQjB6zSMXZ8.summary: Bishop Joshua contrasts the anointing (power of God to do ministry work) with the presence of God (which transforms character and nature). He emphasizes that while the presence of God changes who you are through intimacy with Him, the anointing is the power needed to accomplish God's work and reaches people with the Gospel. The sermon uses the generator/transformer metaphor to explain that increased spiritual capacity and power (anointing) enables greater ministry fruit, comparing Jesus' 30 years of waiting for the anointing before beginning His public ministry to the importance of believers seeking to be filled with the Holy Spirit's power for effective witnessing.

MzkX48DEyBA.title: 7 Steps to the Anointing in  Jordan  - Joshua Heward Mills
MzkX48DEyBA.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills speaking, references his father Bishop Dag Heward-Mills throughout as his spiritual authority figure
topics: Going to Your Man of God
MzkX48DEyBA.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that the anointing of God is accessed by going personally to one's spiritual man of God, using Jesus's journey to John the Baptist as the foundational example. He emphasizes that the anointing is distinct from hard work or wisdom—it is the supernatural power of God that accelerates ministry effectiveness and produces visible spiritual fruit. The critical first step is making a personal decision to seek out and receive the laying on of hands from an anointed servant of God.

g3PCE93HQr8.title: Number One Manifestation of The Holy Spirit - Joshua Heward-Mills
g3PCE93HQr8.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at what appears to be a Bible school or ministerial training setting
g3PCE93HQr8.summary: The first manifestation of the Holy Spirit is His role as teacher and reminder, enabling believers to understand scripture and receive divine instruction and correction. The Holy Spirit transforms people from hopelessness and incompleteness into vessels capable of great ministry and obedience through four primary functions: doctrine, reproof, correction, and instruction. Without the indwelling presence and active teaching of the Holy Spirit, pastors and believers remain incomplete and unable to fulfill God's calling, regardless of their education, gifts, or efforts.

HgMZINOIL24.title: Prayer Is Very Important - Joshua Heward-Mills
HgMZINOIL24.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching on the importance and effects of prayer.
HgMZINOIL24.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that prayer is the most essential spiritual discipline, citing James 5:16 on the effectual prayer of righteous people. He presents eighteen benefits of prayer including becoming a golden vessel, receiving signs and wonders, maintaining the fear of God, and experiencing God's presence and glory in ministry. The sermon emphasizes that consistent daily prayer directly determines spiritual growth, church expansion, and provision, with vivid examples of how prayer transforms churches from ordinary to supernatural.

YxOoANE-0r0.title: The Foundation of Loving God | Foundations of Ministry Camp | Joshua Heward- Mills
YxOoANE-0r0.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at Foundations of Ministry Camp
topics: Loving God more than material possessions
Relationship with God versus relationships with people
YxOoANE-0r0.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that loving God is the foundational requirement for all ministry and Christian service, using Jesus' repeated question to Peter about love as the basis for shepherding believers. He emphasizes that obedience, God's instructions, and all blessings flow from this primary love relationship, and that God is jealous for our complete devotion, testing whether we love Him more than money, careers, relationships, or any other pursuit. The sermon calls believers to emotional, soulful worship and covenant commitment to God above all idols, illustrating how true intimacy with God through sacrificial love produces real spiritual power and transformation.

XTTgWnEINp4.title: Poison V | Holy Ghost Encounter | Joshua Heward-Mills
XTTgWnEINp4.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at his church
XTTgWnEINp4.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills continues the 'Poison' sermon series, focusing on tailbearing and slander as spiritual poisons that damage relationships and communities. He defines tailbearers as people who maliciously gossip and reveal secrets, using personal examples from his pastoral experience to show how gossip destroys even strong relationships and creates strife in churches and offices. He emphasizes that not all secrets should be shared, that mercy should guide believers' responses to others' failures, and that eliminating tale-bearers would eliminate much of the conflict in families and communities.

Lubqkuxe6Yc.title: Poison IV: EXPRESSING DOUBT | Holy Ghost Encounter | Joshua Heward-Mills
topics: Power of confession and spoken faith
Lubqkuxe6Yc.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that expressing doubt is spiritual poison that prevents God's promises from manifesting in our lives, using the example of Israel's 12 spies who brought an 'evil report' of realistic facts instead of faith in God's promise. He emphasizes that true faith requires speaking positive confessions daily despite unfavorable circumstances, and demonstrates how Abraham at 99 changed his name to 'Father of Many Nations' and forced those around him to speak faith into reality. The message calls believers to remove doubt-expressing people from their circles, speak their blessings continuously, and believe God's word more than their own experience or assessment of facts.

Luwtqm7BxJY.title: Poison III: BLASPHEMY | Holy Ghost Encounter | Joshua Heward-Mills
Luwtqm7BxJY.preacherNote: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills (BJosh) preaching at his church
Luwtqm7BxJY.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills preaches on blasphemy as the third poison of the tongue, emphasizing that death and life are in the power of the tongue and what we speak determines our destiny. He explains blasphemy as speaking irreverently or disrespectfully about God, His name, His church, and His servants, and provides the biblical consequence from Leviticus 24 where God commanded stoning for one who blasphemed His name. The sermon warns believers to guard their tongues carefully, refrain from speaking negatively about God's church or pastors, and instead speak life and blessing over their circumstances.

Snx2S5Hsq8Q.title: Poison II: LIES | Holy Ghost Encounter | Joshua Heward-Mills
Snx2S5Hsq8Q.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that lies are a poison that damages your conscience (your internal moral compass), hardens your heart, causes self-deception, and opens the door to numerous other sins. He emphasizes that controlling your tongue and speaking truth is essential for living long, enjoying life, and maintaining spiritual integrity before God. The sermon contrasts authentic Christian living guided by conscience with fake Christianity built on deception and hypocrisy.

wnFC0I7wImg.title: Poison! | Holy Ghost Encounter Service | Joshua Heward-Mills
wnFC0I7wImg.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching at Holy Ghost Encounter Service
wnFC0I7wImg.summary: Bishop Heward-Mills introduces a series on 'Poison' by teaching from James 3 about the power of the tongue to direct one's entire life, using the illustrations of a bit in a horse's mouth and a small helm steering a massive ship. He emphasizes that controlling one's speech is foundational to controlling one's body and life direction, and that words spoken—whether declarations, confessions, or curses—set the trajectory for blessings or destruction. The sermon stresses that believers must be aware of what they speak, both aloud and in their hearts, as the tongue is creative and determines outcomes in marriage, finances, health, and all areas of life.

hnKbMu_FT60.title: The Quiet Time: The Sermon That Became A Conversation | Joshua Heward-Mills
hnKbMu_FT60.preacherNote: Joshua Heward-Mills preaching; transcript includes conversational asides and audience interaction characteristic of his style.
topics: Word of God in the heart
Personal transformation through intimacy with God
Seeking God's will vs. worldly counsel
hnKbMu_FT60.summary: Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that God's constant ambition for believers is not to chase worldly success but to focus on pleasing Him, which paradoxically leads to greater fulfillment and God's vision for their lives. He emphasizes the critical spiritual discipline of 'quiet time'—being alone with God in stillness and silence—as the foundational practice for hearing God's voice, knowing His character, and experiencing transformation. The sermon illustrates through biblical examples (Jacob, Jesus, the woman caught in adultery) that personal spiritual breakthroughs and the deepening of God's word from the mind into the heart occur only in seasons of intentional solitude with God.
